Cloud Solution Architect, Microsoft

Workshop overview · 4 phases

Agenda

From “interested in agents” to a funded, governed pilot — in four delivery phases. Click any phase to jump straight to it.

01

Technical Readiness

Foundations + architecture + capabilities of agents.

  • Mental model · Control System · Spectrum
  • Agent Store · Prebuilt · Partner agents
  • Where agents live · maker experiences
  • Licensing · Anatomy · App package
  • Instructions · Knowledge · Tools · Triggers
  • Decision guide · BYOA · Toolkit & Foundry
02

Art of the Possible

Six hero demos across the maturity spectrum.

  • SharePoint Agents — Retrieve tier
  • Researcher / Analyst — Reason
  • Copilot Studio Lite — Marketing Agent
  • Agent Builder — Employee IT Agent
  • Autonomous — HR triage + PC Request
  • BYOA — 3 recordings
03

Assessment

Turn conversation into a prioritised scenario backlog.

  • Discovery — 9-step listening checklist
  • Persona challenges template
  • Envision — use-case & feature mapping
  • Define Agent Use Case template
  • Prioritisation — Value × Complexity
  • AI Transformation Roadmap
04

Pilot Plan

The deliverable that closes the workshop.

  • Top-2 priority use cases & KPIs
  • Technical requirements checklist
  • Governance — M365, Studio, Purview
  • Up-to-3 next actions & Value Realisation
  • AI Transformation Journey & offerings
  • Practical pointers & pre-pilot checklist
Setting the scene

How we’ll work together

A few starting suggestions — we’ll confirm what works best for your team during the kickoff. None of this is set in stone.

🔴

Recording

Happy to record the session if useful, so you can revisit it later. We’ll agree at the start.

💬

Questions

Hands up or chat — whatever feels natural. Tell us your preference and we’ll roll with it.

🅿️

Parking lot

Anything off-topic but worth keeping — we’ll capture it and follow up after the workshop.

☕

Breaks

Suggested rhythm: ~5 min every hour and a ~45 min lunch. Adjust to suit your day.

🎤

Microphones

Mute when you’re not speaking helps the audio. Chat is great for quick asides while someone else is talking.

🧪

Demo caveat

Demos run on a CDX tenant with sample data. Production may behave slightly differently — we’ll flag anything material.

Set the lens, then build the toolkit. What an agent is, where it lives, how it’s governed — then how you actually ship one.

Copilot and Agents — the smartphone-and-apps analogy

The mental model we'll come back to all day. Microsoft 365 Copilot is the device; agents are the apps.

5 min
Microsoft 365 Copilotthe device
… and your familiar Microsoft appsthe apps
Think of Copilot as your smartphone and agents as its apps. Your smartphone is where you interact with the apps. And there are apps to do all kinds of things, from booking a ride to the airport to managing your finances to helping you learn a new language. Just like apps, agents will be able to do a wide variety of things. In essence, agents are the new apps for an AI-powered world. — Jared Spataro, Chief Marketing Officer, AI at Work @ Microsoft

M365 Copilot & Chat are your modern work assistants — agents make them act on the business

User ↔ Microsoft 365 Copilot / Copilot Chat ↔ Agents. The user always interacts with Copilot or Chat; agents are surfaced inside that conversation through the agents pane, an @mention, or autonomous triggers.

5 min
Microsoft 365 Copilot and Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat are your modern work assistants in the Microsoft ecosystem
User
Microsoft 365 Copilot /
Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat
Agents are new apps for an AI-powered world
User
Microsoft 365 Copilot /
Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat
Agents
AI Assistants can utilize Agents to drive and enhance your business processes
User
Microsoft 365 Copilot /
Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat
✓ ✓ !
Agents
It helps to 📈 grow revenue and 💰 optimise costs
Top-line value

AI assistants leverage agents to drive and enhance business processes, helping grow revenue and optimise costs. This is the slide to anchor the executive conversation.

Copilot Control System — secure & compliant at scale

Copilot, Copilot Chat and Agents can be controlled across the tenant by IT. The Control System spans Microsoft 365 admin centre, Power Platform admin centre and Microsoft Purview.

4 min

Microsoft 365 Copilot, Copilot Chat and Agents can be controlled at scale to stay secure and compliant with your org policies

User
Microsoft 365 Copilot /
Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat
✓ ✓
Agents
Copilot Control System
🛡
Data protection
⚙
Management controls
📈
Measurement and reporting
🛂

Management controls

Make agents available to specific users / groups · block / unblock · deploy / remove · governance UX in M365 admin centre's Integrated apps.

🛡

Data protection

Microsoft Purview — sensitivity labels, DLP, IRM, Communication Compliance, eDiscovery, Audit, Data Lifecycle Management.

📊

Measurement & reporting

Copilot Analytics in Viva Insights · message-consumption views · Power Platform billing · DSPM for AI.

Copilot Control System Manage agents (Integrated apps) Purview for AI

Agent creation tools — low-code or pro-code

Two paths converge in the same Microsoft 365 Apps ecosystem (Teams App Catalog + Microsoft 365 Apps store).

4 min

Agents can be created using low-code tools

User
Microsoft 365 Copilot /
Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat
Copilot Studio(low-code)
Visual Studio(pro-code)

Agents can be created using custom code

Microsoft 365 Copilot is the UI for AI
User
Microsoft 365 Copilot /
Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat
Agents

No-code · Agent Builder in Copilot

Built right inside the Microsoft 365 Copilot app — the Agent Builder lets anyone describe an agent in plain language, point it at a SharePoint site or files, and publish in minutes. No setup, no studio, no developer needed.

  • For: Every employee.
  • Output: Personal & shared declarative agents.

Low-code · Copilot Studio

Studio (full canvas) and Studio Lite (in M365 Copilot). Topics, Generative Orchestration, Tools, Knowledge, autonomous Triggers. SaaS, Power Platform-hosted.

  • For: Makers and end users.
  • Output: Declarative agents and Custom-engine agents.

Pro-code · Visual Studio / VS Code

Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit (formerly Teams Toolkit) + Microsoft 365 Agents SDK. Code-first declarative or custom-engine agents. Full ALM with source control.

  • For: Developers, ISVs.
  • Output: Agents deployable across M365 Copilot + 15 channels.
Microsoft 365 Copilot is the UI for AI

You’re already in Microsoft 365 every day — and that’s exactly where your agents will live. No new app to learn, no separate hub to remember. Whether your team builds with low-code or pro-code, the result lands in the same place: right inside Copilot, ready to help.

Copilot Studio Agents Toolkit Agents SDK Publish & distribute

Agents run on many AI infrastructures — and ship to many channels

Three runtime tiers, virtually unlimited end-points.

4 min
Microsoft 365 Copilot is the UI for AI
User
Microsoft 365 Copilot /
Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat
Agents

Both 3rd party and custom agents can be published and distributed using the Microsoft 365 Apps ecosystem

Agents can run on many different AI infrastructures
User
Microsoft 365 Copilot /
Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat
Agents
Agents can run on…
Microsoft 365 Copilot /
Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat(declarative)

Declarative agents — runtime managed by Microsoft. Bing grounding + Microsoft Graph.

Copilot Studio /
Power Platform(custom low-code)

Custom low-code — bring your own orchestrator, Topics + Generative Orchestration, Power Platform connectors.

Azure AI Foundry /
Azure AI services(custom pro-code)

Custom pro-code — bring your own LLM, custom RAG, your own UX. Best for IP-heavy or model-customised scenarios.

🌐 Channels

Channel

Web & native apps

Embed in your own product UX or website chat widget.

Channel

Popular messengers

Teams, Facebook, Slack (via M365 Agents SDK).

Channel

Voice channels

Telephony / IVR / contact-centre integration.

Channel

Customer engagement hubs

D365 Customer Service, ServiceNow, Salesforce.

Declarative agents Copilot Studio fundamentals Azure AI Foundry Publish to channels

Agents vary complexity and capability — Retrieve → Task → Autonomous

Agents use AI to automate and execute business processes, working alongside or on behalf of a person, team or organisation. Plot every customer scenario on this spectrum during ideation to set realistic expectations on cost, governance and adoption.

6 min

What can agents do?

Agents use AI to automate and execute business processes,
working alongside or on behalf of a person, team or organisation

Retrieval
Task
Autonomous
Simple Advanced
Agents vary complexity and capabilities depending on your need

Spectrum of agents

IT Helpdesk agent

How do I connect to the corporate network?

Device Refresh agent

Request a new laptop and send approvals via IT Service tool.

Lead Gen agent

The agent has identified and researched 15 new leads for you to review.

Simple Advanced

Retrieval

Retrieve information from grounding data, reason, summarise, and answer user questions.

Task

Take actions when asked, automate workflows, and replace repetitive tasks for users.

Autonomous

Operate independently, dynamically plan, orchestrate other agents, learn and escalate.

TypeDescriptionExampleTypical Microsoft surface
Retrieval (Simple)Retrieve information from grounding data, reason, summarise, and answer user questions.IT Helpdesk agent: "How do I connect to the corporate network?"SharePoint agents · declarative agents in Copilot Studio Lite
TaskTake actions when asked, automate workflows, replace repetitive tasks.Device Refresh agent: request a new laptop and send approvals via IT Service tool.Copilot Studio agents with Power Platform connectors / API plugins
Autonomous (Advanced)Operate independently, dynamically plan, orchestrate other agents, learn and escalate.Lead Gen agent: "The agent has identified and researched 15 new leads for you to review."Custom-engine agents in Copilot Studio with autonomous triggers, multi-agent orchestration, optional Foundry models
Start where it matters most

Not every agent needs to be a moon-shot. The best place to start is usually a Retrieval agent that answers your team's everyday questions — quick to build, easy to govern, instant value. As confidence grows, you can move to Task agents that do the work for you, and on to Autonomous agents that run in the background. We'll walk this curve together and pick the tier that fits each idea you bring.

Decision guide Autonomous agents (triggers)

Agent Store — the marketplace inside Microsoft 365 Copilot

A catalogue of agents from Microsoft, partners and customer organisations, surfaced inside Microsoft 365 Copilot and relying on the Microsoft 365 Apps ecosystem.

4 min
What it is

One discovery surface

A place featuring agents from Microsoft, partners and customer organisations — all surfaced in the same agents pane.

Foundation

M365 Apps ecosystem

Agent Store relies on the Microsoft 365 Apps Ecosystem — distributed via the Teams App Catalog & Microsoft 365 Apps store.

Who governs

IT admin in control

Full visibility and control over how agents are deployed, via the M365 admin centre's Integrated apps experience.

Quality bar

App Certification

All agents — Microsoft, partner, custom — undergo strict validation for functionality, security and compliance (M365 App Compliance Program).

Agent Store overview M365 App Certification Manage agents (Integrated apps)

Prebuilt Agents — Microsoft-built, deployable in minutes

Streamline business processes seamlessly across Microsoft 365 apps. All ship with M365 Copilot.

5 min

Reasoning

Researcher

Deep-reasoning models grounded in Copilot Chat web & work data. Strategic briefs, GTM plans and exec analysis without leaving Copilot.

Data

Analyst

OpenAI o3-mini reasoning + Python execution. Turns spreadsheets and raw data into trends, forecasts and advanced analysis.

Meetings

Facilitator

Real-time meeting facilitation in Teams — captures decisions, action items and a live summary while the call is still happening.

Translation

Interpreter

Real-time translation in Teams meetings — even mirrors your own voice in the target language.

Sales

Sales agent

Researches prospects and qualifies leads autonomously, grounded in your D365 + CRM data with pipeline insights.

Sales

Sales chat

Gives sellers an instant view of pipeline, accounts and opportunities directly inside the Copilot chat.

HR

Employee Self-Service

Policy Q&A, benefits and time-off lookup — triggers everyday HR & IT workflows without a help-desk ticket.

SharePoint

SharePoint agents

One-click agent grounded on any SharePoint site or library — instant Q&A over your team's documents.

PM

Project Manager

Plan summarisation, status updates and risk surfacing across your Planner projects.

Skills

Skills agent (Viva)

Personalised skills recommendations from Viva Skills — matches people to projects based on what they have and want next.

Prebuilt agents catalogue Researcher Analyst Facilitator Interpreter

Researcher agent

A thought partner for the work that used to need a highly paid analyst — grounded in your data, your context and the open web.

5 min
Strategist on demand

Like a highly-paid analyst

Gives every employee the kind of capability you'd expect from a highly paid strategist or researcher — without leaving Microsoft 365 Copilot.

How it reasons

Deep reasoning + your data

Combines advanced deep-reasoning models with Copilot Chat's web grounding and work grounding, plus the agent ecosystem, to produce well-reasoned answers to complex prompts.

What you get

Grounded in your context

A thought partner that performs advanced reasoning grounded in your unique context — and gives the whole organisation on-demand access to the kind of work usually reserved for senior hires.

🧭 Pick the right mode for the task

Researcher sits next to two faster Copilot Chat modes. Use this guide so people reach for the right one — not the most powerful one — for the job in front of them.

Copilot Chat

⚡ Instant

Type of task

Quick & straightforward Q&A, summaries, brainstorming.

Depth of reasoning

Direct, rapid answers.

Output format

Brief, concise.

Ideal use case

Everyday queries.

Sample prompt

"Based on prior interactions with [person], give me 5 things top of mind for our next meeting."

Copilot Chat

🧩 Thinking

Type of task

Logical analysis & problem-solving.

Depth of reasoning

Multi-step logical reasoning.

Output format

Structured, well-organised.

Ideal use case

Analytical, structured reasoning.

Sample prompt

"Create side-by-side tables of the top 10 companies by market cap in 2000 vs 2025; analyse the shifts."

Researcher

🔬 Deep reasoning

Type of task

Thorough investigations and reports.

Depth of reasoning

Recursive, exhaustive reasoning + synthesis.

Output format

Comprehensive, detailed reports.

Ideal use case

In-depth research, cross-source synthesis.

Sample prompt

"Build a full go-to-market strategy for [product x] — competitive positioning, internal readiness, lessons learned, external trends."

* GPT-X refers to the latest GPT model available at any given time, e.g. GPT 5.2.

What this unlocks for you

Researcher means your team gets analyst-grade work — competitive analysis, GTM plans, exec briefs — on demand, without sending the request out, waiting weeks, or paying for a consulting engagement. It uses a bit more compute than a normal chat, so we'll keep an eye on usage during the pilot and make sure it lands where it adds the most value.

Analyst agent

Your data scientist on demand — built on OpenAI's o3-mini reasoning model and optimised for advanced data analysis at work.

3 min
What it feels like

Like a skilled data scientist

Analyst thinks like a skilled data scientist, so you can go from raw data to insights in minutes — no waiting on a queue, no half-built spreadsheets.

How it reasons

Chain-of-thought, iteratively

Built on OpenAI's o3-mini reasoning model, it uses chain-of-thought reasoning to progress through problems iteratively — taking as many steps as needed to refine its answer.

Why you can trust it

Runs Python, shows the code

Runs Python to tackle your most complex data queries — and you can view the code it's running in real time to check its work.

Why this matters for your team

No more waiting two hours for someone to build a chart. Anyone can get a clean, verified answer from the data they already have — and because Analyst always shows its working in Python, finance, audit and operations can trust the result and re-use the code.

Analyst agent · Learn

Agents by our partners

A growing marketplace of agents and connectors across every business function — easily integrated into Microsoft 365 Copilot.

3 min
Every Employee
Customer Service
Sales
Finance
Marketing
HR
Legal
IT

With a growing marketplace of Microsoft 365 Copilot agents and connectors spanning across business functions and industries, easily integrate the apps and tools you need into Microsoft 365 Copilot.

Where agents are available

Three surfaces, one Copilot Control System — the same governance, security and admin experience underneath.

4 min
Microsoft 365
Copilot Chat
  • Chat — free, secure AI chat powered by GPT 5 and web grounded.
  • Agents — integrated in chat, paid for on a consumption basis.
  • IT Controls — Enterprise Data Protection & agent management.
Microsoft 365
Copilot
  • Everything in Copilot Chat, plus work-grounded chat over your Microsoft Graph.
  • Copilot in Teams, Outlook, Word, Excel, PowerPoint — your AI assistant for work.
  • Copilot Control System & Copilot Analytics — full governance and insight.
Microsoft Teams and
other channels
  • Custom-engine agents from Copilot Studio publish anywhere your customers are.
  • Live websites, mobile apps, M365 Copilot, Teams, Facebook, Slack and more.
  • Same Copilot Control System governs every channel.

assistant		 Copilot reasons over personal work data (e.g. Outlook, OneDrive, Teams meeting transcripts and chats) —

Agents User Experience

Two interaction patterns — pick the right one for the job. Either the agent owns the screen, or it joins the conversation already in flight.

4 min
Immersive

Immersive Experience

The full experience of the Copilot app in Microsoft 365. Users chat directly with the agent embedded in Microsoft 365 Copilot or Copilot Chat.

In-context

In-context Experience

Users interact in the context of an app. Users can @mention the agent and interact with it directly within the app experience.

Other channels Publish custom-engine agents anywhere your users are

With Copilot Studio you can publish custom-engine agents to multiple platforms or channels — wherever your users (or your customers) already work.

Built in
Copilot Studio
Custom-engine agent
Publish to
🌐
Live websites
📱
Mobile apps
Microsoft 365 Copilot
Microsoft Teams
Messaging apps
➕
More channels

One agent, many surfaces. Build it once in Copilot Studio, ship it everywhere your audience lives.

Invoking agents in M365 Copilot Publish to channels (Copilot Studio)

Two ways your organisation can approach agents

From the bottom up — empower your makers — and from the top down — drive org-led solutions. Most organisations end up doing both, and the ideas meet in the middle.

3 min
Empower
agent makers

Bottom-up. Put authoring tools in the hands of everyone and let great ideas surface from the people closest to the work.

  • Unlock innovation at every level, department & function
  • Make agent authoring experiences available to your audiences
  • Apply new technology to existing problems to optimise
  • Set up guidelines and policies
WhoEvery employee & maker
ToolsAgent Builder · Studio Lite · SharePoint
Best forQuick wins & everyday workflows
Time-to-valueHours → days
Where they meet

Agents

Build, extend or modernise solutions for existing problems to optimise processes — and create new ones.

Org-led
solutions

Top-down. Lead with strategic, business-changing agents that re-shape how a function or the whole organisation runs.

  • Create business-changing agents
  • Uncover business scenarios and define requirements
  • Choose solution path
  • Design architecture and implement your solution
WhoCoE, IT, line-of-business sponsors
ToolsCopilot Studio · Azure AI Foundry · Pro-code
Best forCross-team & mission-critical processes
Time-to-valueWeeks → quarter

Agent maker experiences

A spectrum of authoring tools — from End Users in Microsoft 365 Copilot, through Makers in Copilot Studio, to Developers in VS Code & Azure AI Foundry. Match the maker to the experience, not the other way around.

3 min
For End Users For Makers For Developers
Why this matters for your team

Don't funnel every agent idea through your developers. The fastest organisations let end users own everyday retrieval agents in Copilot Studio Lite or SharePoint, free up your makers to focus on cross-team workflows in Copilot Studio, and reserve your developers for the high-value, custom-engine work in VS Code and Azure AI Foundry.

Copilot Studio Lite in Microsoft 365 Copilot

Build an agent directly from Microsoft 365 Copilot — and seamlessly integrate your organisation's knowledge.

4 min

Build an agent directly from Microsoft 365 Copilot and seamlessly integrate your organisation's knowledge.

👆

Easy to create

Choose your grounding data to create a declarative agent in just a few clicks — no environment setup, governed under the user's Microsoft 365 Copilot licence.

🔗

Easy to share

Just like other Microsoft 365 apps — share the link via email or Teams chat, distributed through the Microsoft 365 Apps ecosystem.

Use existing first-party agents as templates for your own

Pick a starting point from the built-in catalog and customise from there — no blank canvas required.

Copilot Studio Lite Build an agent step-by-step

Agents in SharePoint

Create agents in SharePoint based on selected files — scoped for specific business needs and ready in a couple of clicks.

4 min

Create agents in SharePoint based on selected SharePoint files scoped for specific business needs.

👆

Easy to create

Create an agent right from a SharePoint document library in just a few clicks — no environment setup, permission-trimmed by SharePoint ACLs, citations link back to source files.

🤝

Improve collaboration

Mark agents as Approved and enable a site-level agent that becomes available on a landing page — promoting vetted agents inside the team.

Pre-condition before scaling

Enable SharePoint Advanced Management (SAM) first — the oversharing report and access reviews are essential for keeping Copilot grounding clean. Don't ground an agent on a site you haven't audited.

Get started with SP agents SharePoint Advanced Management Adoption playbook

Copilot Studio templates & Managed Agents

Two ways Microsoft accelerates the maker journey — pre-built starting points to customise, and managed solutions you don't have to maintain.

3 min

Copilot Studio templates (Custom Engine Agents in Copilot Studio)

You can utilise existing agents as a template for your own agents in both Copilot Studio Lite and Copilot Studio full experience.

Managed agents (Custom Engine Agents in Copilot Studio)

Managed Agents are managed solutions published by Microsoft, that can be hosted in your environments.

🛡

Centrally updated

These agents are immutable but are centrally updated by Microsoft — updates can include, but are not limited to, switching to a newer model once one becomes available.

⚡

Zero maintenance

You get Microsoft's best-practice templates plus continuous improvements with no ALM overhead — ideal for HR, Helpdesk and FAQ scenarios everyone needs but no one wants to own.

Copilot Studio authoring Managed solutions in PP

Recent licensing changes

Quick name refresh you'll see across the UI and docs — same metering, new currency name.

2 min
WasCopilot Studio Messages → NowCopilot Credits

What does it mean?

  • Messages (in licensing meaning) in UI and documentation will be changed to Copilot Credits.
  • Message Packs are now known as “Copilot Credit pack subscription licence”.

Why the change — and what got better

Why now

One word. Every product.

Agents now span Microsoft 365 Copilot, Copilot Chat and Copilot Studio — “Messages” only made sense in Studio. Copilot Credits is the same currency wherever an agent runs, so finance and IT no longer need a per-product translation.

What stayed the same

No re-pricing, no migration

The metering, allowances and pack sizes haven’t changed — existing tenants and budgets carry over as-is. If you bought Message Packs, you keep the same value under the new name.

What got better

Plain-language reporting

Admin and Power Platform reports now talk in Copilot Credits end-to-end — easier to chargeback, easier to compare against your M365 Copilot spend, and a clearer story for the people approving the budget.

Copilot Studio billing & licensing

Copilot Credits — the slide that wins finance over

Why most M365 Copilot users will never see a credit charge when using agents in Copilot or Copilot Chat.

5 min

What it is

Copilot Credits are common currency that reflects measure of the time and effort required for an agent to reply or perform a task.

Different agent features like knowledge grounding and usage of tools have different billing rates or Copilot Credits cost.

✨ Most Copilot Credit costs are covered for the users with Microsoft 365 Copilot license, when using an agent in Microsoft 365 Copilot or Copilot Chat.

How a credit is spent

Credits consumed

Standard user (no Microsoft 365 Copilot license)

User No Copilot license
Microsoft 365
Copilot (Chat)
+10 Copilot Credits Billed
Agent 1 SharePoint knowledge
+5 Copilot Credits Billed
Agent 2 Dataverse tool

Each agent feature accumulates — the user is billed 15 credits for this interaction.

All included

User with Microsoft 365 Copilot license

User Licensed with M365 Copilot
Microsoft 365
Copilot (Chat)
10 Copilot Credits Included
Agent 1 SharePoint knowledge
5 Copilot Credits Included
Agent 2 Dataverse tool

All grounding & tool calls are covered by the licence — the user is billed 0 credits.

What it costs per interaction

User of M365 Copilot
or Copilot Chat		 Agent's answer Tenant Graph
Grounding in SPO		 Tool that interacts
with Dataverse		 Cost
Standard user Declarative: included
Custom: 2 credits		 + 10 credits + 5 credits =17
User with Microsoft 365
Copilot license		 Included Included Included =0

Notice that use of different agent features accumulates together.

The CFO question

If you're already paying for M365 Copilot per user, most agent interactions are free. The credit metering kicks in for standard (non-Copilot) users, autonomous triggers, premium prompts and agent-flow actions.

Copilot Studio billing & licensing

Licensing options — pick the path that fits

Four ways to pay for Copilot Studio agents. Mix and match per environment — you don't have to commit to one model for the whole tenant.

6 min

The four paths

Path 1 · Bundled

Copilot Studio in M365 Copilot

$30 per user / month

Unlimited4 credits for licensed users

Best when: all your users already have an M365 Copilot licence and you want everyday agents bundled in.

Ships to M365 Copilot & Copilot Chat

Path 2 · Pay-as-you-go

Copilot Studio PAYG

$0.01 per credit

Pay-as-you-go meter, post-paid2

Best when: you want to start small, prove value, and scale spend with actual usage.

Ships to Any channel incl. Copilot Chat

Path 3 · Pre-paid pack

Copilot Studio Credit Packs

$200 per pack / tenant / month

25,000 credits per pack3

Best when: finance prefers a predictable monthly bill rather than variable PAYG charges.

Ships to Any channel incl. Copilot Chat

Path 4 · Bring your own

Custom agent

Azure billing (estimated)

Bring your own model, your own infra

Best when: IP-heavy or model-customised scenarios that need full control via Azure AI Foundry.

Ships to Any channel incl. Copilot Chat

What's included in each path

Feature BundledCopilot Studio
in M365 Copilot		 Pay-as-you-goCopilot Studio
PAYG		 Pre-paidCopilot Studio
Credit Packs		 Bring your ownCustom agent
Capacity
Credits / tenant / month Unlimited4 Pay-as-you-go2 25,000 / pack3 Estimated
Capabilities
Generative AI ● ● ● ●
Create & publish agents to any channel — ● ● ●
Create agents to customise M365 Copilot ● ● — —
Power Automate agent flows ●6 ● ● —
Standard PP connectors ● ● ● —
Premium & Custom PP connectors — ● ● —
On-prem / cloud data transfer — ● ● —
Dataverse for Copilot Studio ●7 ●7 ●7 —
Managed Environments ●8,9 ●8 ●8 —
  • 1Copilot Credits = common currency.
  • 2PAYG meter for post-pay based on actual credits.
  • 31 pack = 25,000 credits, billed annually.
  • 4Interactive use by authenticated M365 Copilot users included at no additional cost; subject to fair-use limits.
  • 5Agents built on Copilot Studio for Teams / SharePoint / M365 Copilot included with M365 Copilot license.
  • 6Cloud-flow usage up to 250,000 PP Requests / day included.
  • 7Dataverse defaults: 5 GB DB, 20 GB File, 2 GB Log.
  • 8See Managed Environments for licensing.
  • 9Managed Environments enabled only for Copilot Studio features.

Billing rates — what each interaction actually costs

The core meter for every agent feature. Most rates land at zero when a Microsoft 365 Copilot-licensed user invokes them — they only meter for non-licensed users and autonomous triggers running without a user prompt.

5 min

Per-feature billing rates

Feature Base rateBilling rate M365 Copilot userInteractive use1 No user promptAutonomous triggers2
💬Answers
Classic answers Static responses authored by makers through topics 1 credit No charge N/A
Web-grounded answers Dynamically generated using the open web as a knowledge source 2 credits No charge3 2 credits
Generative answers4 LLM-generated, context-aware responses 2 credits No charge 2 credits
🔍Knowledge & tools
Tenant graph grounding4 Microsoft Graph + 3rd-party data via Copilot connectors 10 credits No charge 10 credits
Agent tools4 Action tools, Power Platform connectors, custom tools 5 credits No charge 5 credits
Agent flow actions Power Automate flow steps invoked by the agent — rate per 100 actions 13 credits No charge 13 credits
✨Text & gen-AI tools (per 10 responses5)
Basic Lightweight LLM operations 1 credit No charge 1 credit
Standard Default GPT-class model operations 15 credits No charge 15 credits
Premium Reasoning / GPT-X / image & complex generation 100 credits No charge 100 credits
  • 1Interactive use by authenticated Microsoft 365 Copilot users is included at no additional cost.
  • 2Autonomous triggers are events that automatically initiate an agent (no user prompt).
  • 3Use of declarative agents grounded in web data is free on Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat.
  • 4Each interaction can use multiple rates simultaneously — e.g. graph grounding + generative answer = 12 credits.
  • 51 response = 1,000 tokens for LLM models, 1 image for image processing, 1,000 characters for text processing.
Governance reminder

Autonomous triggers run without a user prompt, so they always bill against the Autonomous triggers column. A scheduled HR triage agent firing every 15 minutes can rack up credits fast — monitor consumption closely and throttle when needed.

Agent Usage Estimator

Forecast monthly Copilot Credit consumption for a single agent based on licensing, agent type and features — free, public, no sign-in required.

3 min

The tool

Copilot Studio Estimator

A friendly web app from Microsoft that turns your assumptions about agent usage into a monthly credit estimate — great for budgeting before you commit to a scenario.

microsoft.github.io/copilot-studio-estimator
🆓Free 🌐Public 🔓No sign-in

How to use it in the workshop

1
Pick the agent type and licensing path

Select declarative or custom, and which of the 4 licensing paths the customer plans to use.

2
Plug in usage assumptions

Active users per month, interactions per user, and which features the agent uses (knowledge grounding, tools, autonomous triggers).

3
Read the monthly credit estimate

Capture the output and paste it into the Pilot Plan governance section as the budget basis.

Note: the estimator provides a monthly credit estimate for a single agent and makes no guarantees of final costs.

Practical tip

Run the estimator with the customer in the room during the Pilot Plan workshop to settle the “how much will this cost?” question before committing to a scenario. Capture the output as part of the Pilot Plan governance section.

Open the estimator Credit management

Anatomy of an agent

There are many capabilities that power an agent to assist users effectively by performing a variety of tasks. Five layers, working together to take a request, reason about it, ground it, and act.

5 min

The five capabilities

Layer 1

User experience

Where the user meets the agent — M365 Copilot, Copilot Chat, Teams, Web and other channels.

Layer 2

Orchestrator

Decides what to do next: tool selection, data governance, safety & Responsible AI sit here.

Layer 3

Foundation models

The reasoning engines — GPT-5, GPT-X, o-series, custom LLMs. The brain behind every answer.

Layer 4

Knowledge

Sources used for Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) — Web, SharePoint, External, Graph.

Layer 5

Tools

How the agent acts — invokes operations in Power Platform, MCP servers, REST APIs and more.

How the layers connect

User ExperienceM365 Copilot, Chat, Teams…
M365 Copilot Copilot Chat Teams Web … other channels
Orchestrator
Tool Selection
Data Governance
Safety & Responsible AI
📚
Knowledge
Web Search
SharePoint
External
MS Graph
🔧
Tools
Power Platform
MCP / REST
Other
✨
General
Understanding Translation Summarization Custom instructions
Foundation Models(GPT-5, GPT-X, o-series, Custom LLMs…)
The big idea

Think of an agent like a small, well-coordinated team. The orchestrator is the project manager that decides what to do next, the foundation model is the thinker, knowledge is the library it consults, and tools are the hands that get things done — all working together so you can simply ask in plain language and get real work done, safely and on your data.

M365 Copilot architecture Responsible AI for Copilot

Declarative agents vs Custom-engine agents

The first question we ask in any solution-design conversation. Get this right and 80% of subsequent decisions follow.

5 min
Declarative agents
Custom engine agents
Build
with
Copilot Studio, Copilot Studio Lite,
SharePoint (no-code and low-code) Visual Studio Code (code-first)
Copilot Studio Studio Lite SharePoint VS Code
Copilot Studio (no-code and low-code) Visual Studio Code (code-first)
Copilot Studio VS Code AI Foundry
Agent
runtime
Microsoft 365 Copilot Microsoft-managed orchestrator & foundation models
Bring your own orchestrator and
foundation models Pick custom LLM/SLM, autonomous triggers
Deploy
across
M365 Copilot Microsoft Teams SharePoint
M365 Copilot Microsoft Teams Web + 10 other channels

Decision Flow

Ask one question — "do any of these apply?"

No → Declarative Stay in the managed lane
  • Users live in M365 / Teams / SharePoint and don't need other channels
  • The default models are good enough — no specific LLM/SLM required
  • The agent only acts when a user asks — no event-based / scheduled triggers
  • You want fastest time-to-value and lowest run-cost
Yes → Custom-engine Bring your own runtime
  • You need autonomous triggers — schedule, webhook or event-based
  • You need a custom or fine-tuned LLM/SLM
  • You must publish to channels beyond M365 / Teams — Web, voice, Slack, customer portal…
  • You need deeper code-level customisation of the orchestration loop
★

Start simple, grow as you need to. The vast majority of great agents fit beautifully inside Microsoft 365 Copilot — quick to build, easy to govern, and instantly available to your team. We’ll only step up to a custom-engine agent when your scenario genuinely calls for it, so you get the right tool for the job without any unnecessary complexity.

Declarative agents Custom-engine agents Decision guide

Agent components, dependencies & the app package

Both agent types — declarative and custom-engine — distribute through the same Microsoft 365 Apps ecosystem. The difference is what's under the bonnet: Microsoft-managed dependencies for declarative, your own runtime for custom-engine.

5 min
Microsoft 365 Apps ecosystem · Teams App Store
Agents distribute like any other Microsoft 365 appApp package contents App Manifest Logo & outline Agent manifest* API plugin JSONs*
Declarative agents
Agents for M365 CopilotNo-code & low-code
Instructions Tools Knowledge
Custom Engine Agents
Low-code agentsCopilot Studio
Instructions Tools Knowledge Triggers
Pro-code agentsVS Code · Agents Toolkit & SDK

Custom instructions, grounding & tools

Preferred SDKs and toolchain

Microsoft-managed dependencies

  • Knowledge Web grounding (Bing) + Microsoft Graph data
  • Orchestrator Microsoft 365 Copilot / Copilot Chat
  • Foundation models M365 Copilot / Chat models

Custom dependencies

  • Knowledge Bing, Graph, External + custom RAG / Vector DB
  • Orchestrator Copilot Studio (generative) or custom (Semantic Kernel, AutoGen, LangChain)
  • Foundation models Azure AI Foundry · custom LLM/SLM
Powered by Microsoft Cloud
Legend: Microsoft-managed Low-code Full custom / code

* Agent manifest and API plugin JSONs are declarative-agent only — custom-engine agents handle these in code.

Declarative agent app package

A declarative agent ships as a .zip uploaded into the Teams App Catalog. Inside are five small files — three describe the app, two describe the agent and its tools.

App Package (*.zip) · uploaded into Teams App Catalog

Manifest
Color icon
Outline
Agent manifest
API plugin
Microsoft 365 CopilotAvailable in the agents pane

App-level

Manifest

A crucial part of any app. Defines the app description, properties, permissions, features, and where they're hosted. Most of the information about an app in the Agent Store and M365 App catalogue is from here.

JSON document that follows Teams App Manifest Schema.

Agent-level

Agent manifest

Describes the agent itself. Contains instructions, info about turned-on knowledge sources, and tools the agent can use — may reference an API plugin manifest.

JSON document that follows Declarative Agent Manifest Schema.

Tool-level · optional

API plugin

Describes tools that can be used by the agent. Helps the orchestrator with reasoning when selecting an API plugin's functions.

JSON document that follows API Plugin Manifest Schema.

Note: The agent manifest and API plugin files are not required for custom-engine agents — those handle their own runtime in code.

Why this matters for your team

Because agents distribute exactly like any other Microsoft 365 app, you don't need a new deployment pipeline, governance model, or app store. Same admin experience, same security review, same Integrated Apps view in the M365 admin centre — your IT team already knows how to ship them.

Declarative agent manifest API plugin manifest Teams app manifest schema

Building blocks of an agent — the four ingredients

Every agent boils down to four small things: Instructions tell it how to behave, Knowledge tells it what to read, Tools let it act, and Triggers let it run on its own. Same recipe whether you're shipping a tiny SharePoint helper or a fully autonomous custom-engine agent.

4 min

Instructions

A prompt that defines how the agent should work to achieve its specific goals

You are Complaints Triage agent. You analyse incoming feedback in chat or sent by emails, recognize their sentiment, potential severity, and summarize. Use 'Create an item' tool to save the results to SharePoint list. Be polite and use professional tone.

Knowledge

Data that the agent uses to provide responses, based on the context of the requests

You can search for details in…

SharePoint sites
  • contoso.sharepoint.com/sites/policies
  • contoso.sharepoint.com/sites/triage

Tools

Operations that the Agent performs to execute tasks in Line-of-Business (LOB) systems

You can save feedback details in the following format: Title: **email subject or short 1-2 word summary of the feedback** Sentiment: Positive, Negative, Neutral Description: short 2-3 sentence summary. Potential severity: 1(urgent), 2, 3

SharePoint connectorCreate an item

Triggers*

Ability to react on events from different systems

Payload instructions: Use Subject and Body of incoming feedback email

Outlook connectorWhen an email arrives

* Triggers are exclusive to custom-engine agents in Copilot Studio — declarative agents do not support them.

Why this matters for your team

Every agent we build together follows this same simple recipe — so once your team understands these four pieces, they can read, review or improve any agent in your tenant. Same vocabulary, same building blocks, every time.

Instructions — shaping behaviour of the agent

Instructions are the agent's job description. They tell it who it is, what it can (and can't) do, and the style it should use. Get this right and the rest of the agent works almost on autopilot.

8 min

Instructions

Instructions are a set of guidelines and commands that define how an agent should behave. They help to:

  • Define behaviour — establish how the agent should respond to user requests
  • Ensure accuracy — guide the agent in handling data correctly and consistently
  • Maintain workflow — help the agent follow a structured process, enhancing user experience
  • Facilitate customisation — allow developers to tailor the agent's functionality to specific needs
Example: Instructions for Device Troubleshooting Agent 
Identify the device: "What device are you having trouble with?"

Ask for the issue description: "Can you describe the problem you're experiencing?"

Check for basic solutions: "Have you tried restarting the device?"

Guide through specific troubleshooting steps:
  "If the device is not charging, check the power cable."
  "If the screen is frozen, try holding the power button for 10 seconds."

Suggest further action: "If the issue persists, would you like to contact customer support?"

Key Components of Instructions

Every set of instructions is built from three layers: a Purpose that frames the role, Guidelines that shape behaviour and tone, and Skills that describe each concrete ability.

Purpose
Guidelines
General directions Tone Restrictions Terminology
Skills
Skill 1
Description
  • Step-by-step workflow
  • Sample output
Skill 2
Description
  • Step-by-step workflow
  • Sample output
Skill 3
Description
  • Step-by-step workflow
  • Sample output
Skill n
…and so on for every ability

Purpose

Purpose clearly defines the role, area of expertise and primary functions of the agent.

Financial Advisor Agent 
You are a personal finance advisor. Your primary role is to help users manage their budgets and provide investment advice tailored to their financial goals.

Guidelines

Guidelines outline the communication style of the agent. This can range from being concise and straightforward to detailed, interactive, or suggestive. They should also specify any limitations or restrictions that need to be followed.

Guidelines contain 3 elements:

  • General Directions
  • Tone
  • Restrictions

Other additional info can be included as part of guidelines, e.g., organisation-specific term definitions.

Financial Advisor Agent 
Communicate in a friendly and approachable tone. Avoid technical jargon unless the user requests detailed explanations. Avoid sharing external links. Where appropriate, reference the last 12 months financial records.
General direction:

Prioritize clarity and brevity in responses. Always aim to answer questions directly and provide relevant responses when applicable.

Tone:

Communicate in a friendly and approachable tone. Avoid technical jargon unless the user requests detailed explanations.

Restrictions:

Avoid sharing external links. Where appropriate, reference the last 12 months financial records.

Skills

Skills can also be defined in instructions.

They are specific abilities and tasks the agent is equipped to perform. These skills enable the agent to provide relevant advice, analyse information, and guide users through various business processes.

Financial Advisor Agent
Budget Analysis:

The agent can help users review their income and expenses, identify spending patterns, and suggest adjustments to improve their budget.

Investment Recommendations:

The agent can provide tailored investment advice based on the user's financial goals, risk tolerance, and current market trends.

Expense Tracking:

The agent can assist users in tracking their expenses over time, helping them stay within their budget and identify areas for potential savings.

Instructions lifecycle

The process for creating and refining agent instructions. Iterate quickly: write a small set of instructions, test against real prompts, and tighten the wording until the agent does what you actually meant.

Create instructions
for the agent
Publish the
agent
Update
instructions
↑ NO
Outcome
achieved?
Test the
agent
↑ feedback loop
Yes → Success

If "no" → loop back through Update instructions → Test the agent until the outcome is reached.

Examples

✕ Avoid
Provide investment advice.
✓ Use Instead
Suggest three investment options based on the user's risk tolerance: conservative, moderate, and aggressive.
Rule of thumb

Be specific, measurable and bounded — say what to do, what not to do, and what success looks like. The clearer the brief, the better the agent. Don't worry about getting it perfect first time — instructions are made to be tweaked, and we'll iterate together during the pilot.

Instruction best practices Copilot Studio prompt best practices Test the agent

Knowledge — grounding responses in data

Five sources to ground an agent. Most pilots use SharePoint + 1-2 Copilot Connectors.

6 min

Knowledge sources for agents

Knowledge
🌐
Web Search

Results from the internet

SharePoint

Internal pages and documents

Copilot Connectors(formerly Graph Connectors)

External LOB data indexed by Microsoft 365 Search

Dataverse / Dynamics 365

Business data and file uploads directly to the agent

Power Platform Connectors*

Retrieval operations as a knowledge source

* available only in custom-engine agents

Example: Retrieval agent with knowledge

1 · Instructions 2 · Plan 3 · Outcome
Agent follows your instructions
Instructions
"You assist with onsite repair visits. You provide step-by-step instructions based on product knowledge."
Agent completes the ask using your data and knowledge
Activity

Search sources · Knowledge

📄 Product information
Microsoft 365 Search
Agent generates a response
Agent
"Check out step-by-step guide for the error code you shared [1]:"
File in SharePoint.PDF (link)

Citations are mandatory for trust.

Knowledge source

🌐

Web SearchResults from the internet

  • Utilise the power of public internet search to fuel your agent responses.
  • No permissions required — results are available to any user.
  • Relies on the Bing Search service.
  • In custom-engine agents you can scope web search to a curated list of public websites.

Knowledge source

SharePointInternal pages and documents

  • Tap into document libraries or specific documents on SharePoint sites.
  • Users can only see data they have access to (based on SharePoint permissions).
  • Relies on Microsoft 365 Search.
  • Enable SharePoint Advanced Management (SAM) first — its oversharing reports and access reviews are essential for Copilot grounding hygiene.

Knowledge source

Dataverse / Dynamics 365Business data and direct file upload

  • Tap into business data stored in Dataverse and Dynamics 365.
  • Direct File Upload knowledge source — files are uploaded into Dataverse for the agent to use.
  • Relies on Dataverse Search (must be enabled in the environment).

Knowledge source · custom-engine only

Power Platform Connectors as knowledgeRetrieval operations as a source

  • When creating custom-engine agents in Copilot Studio, some Power Platform connectors can be used as a knowledge source.
  • Currently available options: Azure AI Search, Zendesk, Azure SQL.
  • Not available for declarative agents — declarative agents use Power Platform connectors only as tools.
Pre-condition before you scale

Don't ground an agent on a site you haven't audited. Enable SharePoint Advanced Management (SAM) and run its oversharing reports + access reviews before letting Copilot index sensitive content.

Rule of thumb

Start with SharePoint for any internal-document scenario. Add Copilot Connectors when the source of truth lives outside Microsoft 365 (Confluence, ServiceNow, Jira, Salesforce…). Reach for Power Platform connectors as knowledge only when you're building a custom-engine agent and need Azure AI Search, Zendesk or Azure SQL.

Add knowledge SharePoint Advanced Management Azure AI Search Dataverse knowledge

Copilot Connectors (formerly Graph Connectors)

Bring external knowledge into Microsoft 365 Search so it shows up in Copilot, search experiences and agents — with permissions honoured at index and query time.

6 min

Copilot Connectors (formerly Graph Connectors)

Copilot Connectors help Microsoft 365 Search to index items from external systems, so they're available via Copilot and search experiences in Microsoft 365.

Microsoft Graph
Indexed Items Copilot Connector External system
"Can you give me guidance on operational efficiency?"
Agent
Large Language Model
Microsoft 365 boundary

When to use Copilot Connectors?

📊
Unstructured data
📄
Long-form texts that the LLM can summarise
🎯
Unpredictable prompts that need to rely on semantic matching
🐢
Low-activity updated items

Otherwise — for relational data, real-time read/write, or sub-minute refresh — reach for a Power Platform connector (tool) instead.

Value of Copilot Connectors

1

Access enterprise knowledge from 3rd-party data sources, whether on-prem or in the cloud

2

Ingest content into Microsoft Graph to enhance the knowledge layer in Copilot and Agents

3

Search content across Copilot, Microsoft Search, Context IQ and other apps with semantic ranking advantage

4

Data is protected by the same security and compliance policies that apply to Microsoft 365 data

Good candidates for indexing with Copilot Connectors

📄
Document repositories
📚
Knowledge bases
🌐
Web sites
📦
Content management systems

Copilot Connectors and Agents

  • Copilot Connectors can be used both independently and within agents.
  • Connectors can be added to the capabilities of an agent, allowing the agent to access and utilise enterprise knowledge.
  • They allow agents to tap into a wealth of information beyond the confines of Microsoft 365.
  • You can scope an agent's knowledge to specific Copilot Connectors.
  • Searching data indexed by Copilot Connectors benefits from semantic ranking.

Connector experience · 1 of 3

Copilot Connector Experience in Microsoft 365 Copilot

  • Users can find, summarise and learn from line-of-business data through natural-language prompts with Copilot Connectors and Microsoft 365 Copilot.
  • Users can hover over in-text citations to preview external items referenced in Copilot or agent responses.
  • Users can click reference links at the bottom of the response to dive deeper into the referenced content.

Connector experience · 2 of 3

Copilot Connector Experience in Microsoft Search

  • When you use Copilot Connectors to add data sources, your users are able to search for them in the All vertical by default.
  • Users see results from connector data sources merged inline with all the other results from Microsoft apps in SharePoint.
  • Copilot connectors honour the source permissions as configured in your content source.
  • You can customise and configure search results.

Connector experience · 3 of 3

Copilot Connector Experience for Context IQ

  • Context IQ in Outlook on the web with Microsoft Copilot Connectors lets users find in-content suggestions from third-party data while composing emails in Outlook on the web.
  • Editors using Context IQ surface relevant people, files, and Microsoft Copilot Connector content that users can select and embed as an adaptive card within the email.
When to choose what

Reach for a Copilot Connector when you need to ground on unstructured, long-form, semantically searchable content that doesn't change every minute. Reach for a Power Platform Connector (tool) when you need relational/transactional data, real-time read or write actions, or sub-minute refresh.

Connectors overview Use as agent knowledge Connectors gallery

Available Microsoft-built connectors — and how to build your own

30+ Microsoft-built connectors out of the box across Cloud, Preview and On-premises. Build custom ones with the Microsoft Graph Connectors API.

6 min

Available Copilot Connectors by Microsoft

Cloud
ServiceNow
Salesforce
Jira Cloud
Confluence Cloud
CSV
Media Wiki
ADLS Gen2
Azure SQL
Azure DevOps
Enterprise Sites (Cloud)
Preview
GitHub
PostgreSQL
WordPress
Google Drive
Zendesk
Stack Overflow
Zoom
Jira Data Center
SharePoint Server
Tableau Cloud
Adobe Experience Manager
Azure File Shares
Dropbox
GitLab
Asana
Miro
Veeva Vault
Smartsheet
Guru
Seismic
On Premises
Oracle SQL
File Share
SQL Server
Confluence Data Center
Enterprise Sites (On-prem)

Reference: many more connectors created by our partners — Microsoft Copilot connectors gallery | Microsoft Learn

Connector deep-dive · 1 of 4

ServiceNow
Knowledge
  • Connects the ServiceNow Knowledge Base with Microsoft Graph, making knowledge articles searchable within Microsoft 365.
  • Users can quickly find relevant ServiceNow knowledge articles and reason about them in Copilot, improving productivity and reducing the need to switch between platforms.
  • You can set the search permissions to either Everyone or Only people with access to this data source.
  • ServiceNow Catalog and Tickets are available as separate connectors.
Instructions snippet
Find all knowledge articles related to ITIL best practices
in ServiceNow and summarize in 5 bullet points.

Reference: ServiceNow Knowledge Microsoft Copilot connector | Microsoft Learn

Connector deep-dive · 2 of 4

Jira Cloud
  • The Jira Connector helps index content from Jira so end users can search for Jira issues within Copilot and Microsoft Search.
  • You can choose to index either the entire Jira site or specific projects.
  • Filters can be applied using JQL (Jira Query Language) to index specific issue types.
  • Search permissions can be set to Everyone or Only people with access to Jira.
Instructions snippet
• Show me all open JIRA issues assigned to me.
• Provide a brief overview of the top 5 high-priority JIRA
  issues in the 'Backend Development' project.

Reference: Jira Cloud Microsoft Copilot connector | Microsoft Learn

Connector deep-dive · 3 of 4

Enterprise
Websites
  • Enterprise Websites connector helps index and find content from company-owned websites, making it accessible through Microsoft Search.
  • Supports search permissions visible to everyone — indexed data appears in search results and is visible to all users in the organisation.
  • Connector will use the sitemap for crawling if available. If not, it will perform a deep crawl.
  • You can index up to 50 different site URLs in a single connection.
  • Has cloud and on-premises options.
Instructions snippet
Summarize the key points of the latest Customer Success
Stories section on our enterprise website.

Reference: Enterprise Websites Microsoft Copilot connector | Microsoft Learn

Connector deep-dive · 4 of 4

Azure DevOps
Wiki
  • The Azure DevOps Wiki Connector helps index and find content from Azure DevOps.
  • You can index either the entire organisation or specific projects within the selected organisation.
  • To allow the connector to connect to your Azure DevOps Organisation, you must enable Third-party application access via OAuth.
Instructions snippet
List the main insights from the 'Retrospective Meetings'
section of our Azure DevOps Wiki and draft an email to
share that with my team.

Reference: Azure DevOps Microsoft Copilot connector for Microsoft Search and Microsoft 365 Copilot | Microsoft Learn

Build your own

Custom Copilot Connectors

You can build custom connectors with Microsoft Graph to integrate with services that aren't available as connectors built by Microsoft.

You can use the Microsoft Copilot connectors API to:

  • Create and manage external data connections
  • Define and register the schema of the external data types
  • Ingest external data items into Microsoft Graph
  • Sync external groups

For on-premises sources

Graph Connector Agent

  • Some of your data sources, such as File Share, Websites and SQL Server, might be running on-premises.
  • Graph connector Agent (GCA) software enables secure data retrieval from on-premises data sources for indexing in Microsoft 365.
  • Supports full and incremental crawl scheduling to keep the search index current.
  • Is not renamed to "Copilot Connector Agent" yet.
Azure SQL
Salesforce
ServiceNow
ADLS Gen2
Media Wiki
Cloud
Enterprise Websites
Oracle
File Share
SQL Server
On-Premises Graph Connector Agent
Search Index Microsoft 365

Reference: Microsoft Graph connector agent | Microsoft Learn

Two ways to extend the catalogue

If a Microsoft-built connector doesn't fit, you have two paths: (1) browse the partner gallery for hundreds of community-built options, or (2) build your own with the Microsoft Graph Connectors API. For on-prem sources you'll always need the Graph Connector Agent in your network.

Full connectors gallery ServiceNow Knowledge Jira Cloud Connectors API Graph Connector Agent

Connector concepts & building blocks — Connection · Schema · Indexed Items

The mental model behind every Copilot Connector: five concepts to know, and three building blocks your code targets.

4 min

Copilot Connector Concepts · 1 of 2

Copilot Connector
Concepts

Connector

A connector traverses an external data source and returns its data. A connector is mapped to specific data sources, such as Jira Cloud.

Crawling

Process of accessing, retrieving and indexing data from an external source to make it searchable within the Microsoft 365 ecosystem.

Index

Process of making data searchable and accessible in services like Microsoft Search, Microsoft 365 Apps and Microsoft 365 Copilot by connecting and indexing data from third-party sources via Copilot connectors.

Copilot Connector Concepts · 2 of 2

Copilot Connector
Concepts

Access control lists (ACLs)

Access control lists (ACLs) determine which users in your organisation can access each indexed item.

Refresh Settings

Refresh settings determine how often data is synced between the data source and Microsoft Search, with options for full or incremental refreshes based on data modifications.

Building blocks

Copilot Connector Building Blocks

Connection
Use Microsoft Admin Center to create connection and register connection settings.
+
Schema
Register your schema, a JSON definition of metadata properties along with aliases, semantic labels, and more.
+
Indexed Items
Index external items and activities. Apply appropriate control for users and groups to define access to items.

Building block · 1 of 3

Connection

  • A connection is an instance of a connector that connects to a specific external data source.
  • A tenant can have multiple connections. Example: a tenant may have 2 file-share connections or 2 Enterprise Web Connections, etc.
  • Admins manage all tenant connections via Microsoft Admin Center.
  • Each tenant can create up to 30 connections.

Building block · 2 of 3

Schema

  • Schema is a flat list of all the properties that you plan to add to the connection along with their attributes, labels and aliases.
  • Schema defines the structure and properties of data to be indexed and made searchable within Microsoft 365.
  • You must register the schema before adding items into the connection.

Building block · 3 of 3

Indexed Items

  • Items ingested by your connector to Microsoft 365 are represented by the externalItem resource (external item API) in Microsoft Graph.
  • externalItem includes properties like acl (access control list), content (the main data content), id (unique identifier), and properties (additional metadata about the item).
{
  "acl": [
    {
      "type": "user",
      "value": "user-id-1234",
      "accessType": "grant"
    },
    {
      "type": "group",
      "value": "engineering-team-id",
      "accessType": "grant"
    }
  ],
  "content": {
    "@odata.type": "microsoft.graph.externalConnectors.externalItemContent",
    "value": "This is the content of the external item, such as the text of a document."
  },
  "id": "external-item-5678",
  "properties": {
    "@odata.type": "microsoft.graph.externalConnectors.properties",
    "title": "Sample External Item",
    "createdDateTime": "2024-07-25T14:30:00Z",
    "lastModifiedDateTime": "2024-07-25T16:00:00Z",
    "priority": 1,
    "status": "open",
    "tags": ["project-x", "important"]
  }
}

JSON representation of external item

The recipe for any custom connector

Whenever you build a custom Copilot Connector, you'll always do these three things in order: create a Connection (Admin Center) → register a Schema (JSON) → ingest Indexed Items (externalItem API). The five concepts above just describe what's happening at each step.

Schema definition Manage indexed items & ACLs API limits externalItem resource

Tools — how an agent acts on the business

Five categories of tools an agent can call. Same agent can mix them — pick the right tool per task.

5 min

Tools available to an agent

Tools
Prompt

AI prompt powered by AI Builder

Power Platform
Connectors

Connect to over 1,400+ internal and external services, including custom

API Plugin (REST API)

Flexible and scalable way to connect your agent to custom APIs

Model Context Protocol

Open standard designed with AI in mind

Agent Flows*

Enable predictable automations powered by Power Automate

* available only in custom-engine agents

Example: Task agent with tool usage

1 · Instructions 2 · Plan 3 · Outcome
Agent follows your instructions
Instructions
"HR agent to help answer common HR-related questions and help with common tasks like requesting vacation or checking vacation balances. Keep it professional, but friendly."
Agent completes the ask using available tools
Activity · Use a tool
⚙ Operate on vacation requests
⚙ Check vacation balance
Call a tool
Agent generates a response
Agent
"In the last 30 days, you used 2 vacation days and have 17 remaining. You can check your vacation requests here."

Tool deep-dive

Prompt

Create a prompt template that can be used by the agent as a tool.

Multiple models are available currently

Basic GPT-4.1 mini — default for everything. Fast, cheap.
Standard GPT-4.1 or GPT-5 — quality-sensitive content (drafts, summaries).
Premium o3 or GPT-5 reasoning (paid preview) — multi-step reasoning, complex analysis.

Code interpreter can be turned on for such prompts.

It's also possible to tap into models hosted in Azure AI Foundry.

Rule of thumb

Knowledge sources let an agent read; tools let it act. Reach for a Prompt when the work is pure language. Reach for a Power Platform Connector or API Plugin when the agent needs to query or update a system. Reach for an Agent Flow when the path must be deterministic — and reach for MCP to future-proof against new AI-native tool sources.

Add tools (Copilot Studio) AI Builder prompts BYO models

Power Platform Connectors & API Plugins

The two biggest paths to action in line-of-business systems — pre-built connectors for breadth, custom API plugins for depth.

4 min

Tool deep-dive · 1 of 2

Power Platform Connectors

  • Power Platform connectors enable agents to interact with external systems and retrieve data or perform actions.
  • Power Platform connectors provide a broader reach, allowing agents to access and interact with a wider range of Microsoft 365 services, external services, and data sources.
+ more
1,400+ connectors

Tool deep-dive · 2 of 2

API Plugins

  • Flexible and scalable way for your agent to connect with and use data.
  • Use OpenAPI definition to quickly connect agent to your API.
Microsoft Graph
"Summarize the latest updates on the Fabrikam project"
Agent
API Plugin
Large
Language Model
Microsoft 365 boundary
When to choose which

Power Platform connector — first choice if it exists. Governed, throttled and audited via Power Platform admin centre, with environment / DLP policies.
API Plugin — first choice for pro-dev teams that already own an API and want full ALM via VS Code + the M365 Agents Toolkit.

Connector reference Use connector as tool API plugins Build an API plugin

Model Context Protocol & Agent Flows

The emerging open standard (MCP) and the deterministic workhorse (Agent Flows) — two more tools an agent can call.

5 min

Tool deep-dive · 1 of 2

Model Context Protocol (MCP)

Model Context Protocol (MCP) allows users to connect with existing knowledge servers and data sources directly within Copilot Studio.

Connecting to an MCP server gives you access to

  • Resources — file-like data that client applications can read (like API responses or file contents).
  • Tools — functions a language model can call.
  • Prompts — predefined prompt templates to accomplish specific tasks.

Why it matters: using MCP future-proofs your tool integrations across Copilot Studio, VS Code, Claude Desktop and other MCP-compatible hosts.

paths:
  /sse:
    get:
      summary: MCP Server Actions
      parameters:
        - in: query
          name: sessionId
          type: string
          required: false
      produces:
        - application/json
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Immediate Response
          schema:
            $ref: '#/definitions/QueryResponse'
        '201':
          description: Created and will follow callback
      operationId: InvokeMCP
      tags:
        - Agentic
        - McpSse
securityDefinitions: {}
security: []

Tool deep-dive · 2 of 2

Agent Flows

Agent Flows are deterministic workflows that leverage predefined action sequences based on Power Automate flows.

This ensures that tasks always run consistently and rapidly.

Ideal for highly repetitive steps — agent flows can significantly accelerate workflow execution.

Note: agent flows are exclusive to custom-engine agents in Copilot Studio.

When to choose which

Reach for MCP when you want to plug an agent into existing AI-native servers (file systems, dev tools, data sources) using an open standard that works across hosts. Reach for an Agent Flow when the path must be deterministic — same inputs, same steps, same outputs every time.

MCP introduction MCP server catalogue Add MCP server to Copilot Studio Agent flows

When & why to use tools — and where to build them

The decision framework that closes the Tools chapter: when a tool beats grounding, the value tools unlock, and where to author them.

4 min

Decision framework · 1 of 3

When to use tools

Actual data without waiting for indexing
More precise prompts predicted
Highly relational data
Read and write actions
More complex scenarios that require architecture with additional components
Already have an API to plug into

Decision framework · 2 of 3

Value of tools

1

Connect Copilot to third-party applications

2

Retrieve actual business data without waiting for indexing

3

Assist users with tools using rich customisable adaptive cards

4

Re-use existing investments from the Microsoft ecosystem or build new

Decision framework · 3 of 3

Building tools for agents

Copilot Studio

  • Familiar with Low-code SaaS-based tooling
  • Have existing Power Platform solutions
  • Rapid development environment
  • Managed infrastructure
  • Supports prompts (former AI Builder) and agent flows

Agents Toolkit

  • Familiar with managed code
  • Have existing Teams apps
  • Multi-tenant deployment
  • Source control, branching, release management practices
  • Need to manage hosting infrastructure yourself
How to choose

If your makers are low-code people who already live in Power Platform, build tools in Copilot Studio. If your devs are pro-code people who already ship Teams apps with branches, releases and source control, build tools in the M365 Agents Toolkit for VS Code. The same agent can use tools authored in either.

Adaptive cards in Copilot Agents Toolkit overview Copilot Studio overview

Triggers & autonomous agents

Available only for custom-engine agents in Copilot Studio, not for declarative agents. Triggers are how an agent runs without a user prompt — and the foundation of autonomous and multi-agent scenarios.

5 min

Triggers · 1 of 2

Triggers (Custom Engine Agents in Copilot Studio)

Triggers are not available for Declarative agents, but available for custom engine agents in Copilot Studio.

Triggers can enable autonomous agent and multi-agent scenarios.

Triggers · 2 of 2

Example: Autonomous agent with a trigger

1 - Trigger 2 - Instructions 3 - Plan 4 - Outcome
Agent automatically reacts to triggers
When a request comes as an email ⚡
Agent follows your instructions
HR agent to help answer common HR-related questions and help with common tasks like requesting vacation or checking vacation balances. Keep it professional, but friendly.
Agent completes the ask using your data and knowledge
Activity:
Search knowledge and use a tool
⚙ Operate on vacation requests
⚙ Check vacation balance
📄 Contoso HR policies
Searches for relevant data and executes a tool
Agent generates a response and saves it in target system
Plan for the cost of "always-on" agents

Because autonomous agents run on their own — without anyone typing a prompt, they can quickly add up. A simple agent set to check every 15 minutes will run around 96 times a day, and each run consumes Copilot capacity. Start with a sensible schedule, watch usage in the first few weeks, and tighten the trigger if it's busier than expected.

About triggers Add an event trigger

Custom-engine agents — pro-code

When the out-of-the-box experience isn't enough, pro-code custom-engine agents let you swap in your own LLM, orchestrator, knowledge layer, UX and management. Same building blocks — full control of every layer.

5 min

Custom Engine Agents – 'Pro code'

The pro-developer path: bring your own model, your own orchestrator, your own UX — and ship to channels well beyond Microsoft 365.

A few words about custom engine agents in 'pro code'…

HR
agent
IT
agent
PM
agent
Legal
agent
Tutor
agent
Custom agents =
Your custom AI stack

Your custom LLM + Orchestrator
+ Grounding + UX + Management + …

Declarative agents vs Custom Engine agents (pro code)

Partners Microsoft
Declarative Agents
Agent prompts
Microsoft 365 Copilot
Custom tools, APIs
Copilot connectors
+
M365
Microsoft 365 Copilot
Workflow instructions
Triggers
Actions / Tools
Knowledge
Orchestrator
Foundation Models
⚙
+
◎
Custom Engine Agents (pro code)
Custom prompts & dialogs
Custom intent detection
AutoGen
Custom tools, APIs
Custom RAG w/ Vector DB
Graph Knowledge via TCP
Custom Planners i.e. LangChain
Semantic Kernel
Any LLM, SLM
Azure AI Foundry

Decision criteria

Why 'pro code' Custom Engine Agents?

Model choice

Bring your own LLM or SLM

You need a specific open-source or custom model instead of the one Microsoft 365 Copilot ships with.

Example: a regulated bank fine-tunes Phi-4 on its own loan policies and serves it from Azure AI Foundry.

Audience

Reach users outside Microsoft 365

Your customers, partners or citizens aren't licensed for M365 — but still need to talk to your agent.

Idea: a public-sector benefits chatbot embedded on the gov.uk portal, anonymous access via Entra External ID.

Experience

Build a fully custom UX

Embed the agent in your own app, web portal or mobile — alongside (or instead of) Microsoft 365 hubs.

Example: an in-app concierge inside the retailer's React-Native shopping app, branded end-to-end.

Compliance

Tighter control over the experience

You need custom moderation, regulatory frameworks or sector-specific legal & compliance gates the declarative path can't express.

Idea: a healthcare agent that blocks PHI patterns and routes flagged turns to a human reviewer.

Architecture

Full control of the stack

You want to own every layer: Prompt Management, Tools, Triggers, Planner and Orchestration — typically with LangChain, Semantic Kernel or AutoGen.

Example: a multi-agent research assistant with a custom planner that fans out across 6 specialised agents.

Reuse

Carry forward existing bots

You already have existing bots or deterministic flows for pre-defined workflows that you don't want to rewrite.

Idea: migrate a Bot Framework v4 bot into the Agents SDK and add a small LLM layer on top.

Distribution

Publish as a SaaS product

You want to publish to Microsoft Stores as a SaaS provider and reach customers via Microsoft AppSource & Teams Store.

Example: an ISV ships a "Contract Reviewer" agent as a transactable SaaS offer with monthly subscription.
Same building blocks, full control

Both paths use the same six layers. Declarative agents fill them with Microsoft 365 Copilot out of the box — quick to ship, easy to govern. Pro-code custom-engine agents let you swap any layer for your own choice when the standard recipe just won't fit. Our recommendation: start declarative. Only step up to pro-code when one of the seven reasons above is a real, written-down requirement — not a "nice to have".

Custom-engine agents Agents SDK Semantic Kernel Azure AI Foundry

Choosing an agent implementation path

Two views of the same decision: a simple decision guide that maps the customer's intent to a product family, and a scenarios × tools × audiences map for matching the right authoring tool to the right maker.

5 min

Decision guide · 1 of 2

Simple decision guide

I want a generative AI solution
I want to build a solution with custom data and UI and deploy internally or externally
I want out-of-the-box solutions that work with my existing data estate for my employees
I want full control over PaaS services and customise with code
I want a SaaS tool to build with natural language and GUI
I want insights and actions on Microsoft 365 data and products
I want role-specific insights and actions for sales, service and financial professionals that integrate with external CRM and ERP systems and connect data to Microsoft 365 productivity apps

(+ Copilot Chat)

Mapping together: scenarios, tools, and audiences

Out-of-the-Box Customised
Copilot Studio Lite
Copilot Studio
Copilot Studio / TTK
Personal Productivity
Business Process
Organization Value
Users
Users
Makers
Makers
Developers
  • Unlock productivity
  • Easy to build
  • Make teams more effective
  • Part of tools you know & love
  • Use custom Instructions
  • Share these agents with others

  • Horizontal workflows

  • More powerful with low/no code
  • Advanced Automation

  • Hand off to live agent / human
  • Customer-facing agents

  • Dynamic actions
  • Publish inside or outside M365
How to use these two views

Use the simple decision guide first — it shows which product family fits what you want to do, and how it's billed. Then use the mapping together view to choose the right tool for who'll be building. Most teams start in the middle with Copilot Studio: a sweet spot for makers who want low-code power without giving up control. Choose Copilot Studio Lite for quick personal-productivity wins, or step up to Copilot Studio + Agents Toolkit when your developers need the full pro-code experience.

Decide agent type Copilot Studio overview Agents Toolkit overview

Bring Your Own Agents

Meet the customer where they already work. Anchored on Data – Security – Governance: your solution, your agents, partner agents and Microsoft agents — all surfacing inside Microsoft Copilot Chat.

5 min

Bring Your Own Agents

Bring agents from Copilot Studio, Azure AI Foundry or any provider into Copilot — so users get one place to talk to all the agents that matter to your business.

Bringing all agents inside Microsoft Copilot Chat
