Six reasons

Why Connect your Agents to Microsoft 365 Copilot

  • 👥
    Reach more users across Microsoft 365 Copilot and Microsoft 365.
  • ⌕
    Help increase agent adoption through enhanced discoverability.
  • 🚀
    Accelerate development for multi-platform and multi-modal agents with the Microsoft UX.
  • 🔒
    Securely ground your agent in work data via Microsoft Graph.
  • ⚙
    Help boost productivity with multi-agent orchestration.
  • 🛡
    Extend data protection & management controls to user interactions with agents.
Copilot
Agents
A
B
C
D
E
F

Customer conversation framework

Why Bring Your AI Agents into Copilot?

Customer Pain Points & Motivations

Real-world examples of workflow inefficiencies (switching between tools, manual data transfer).

Unique customer challenges and the need for tailored solutions.

Importance of addressing specific blockers and pain points per account.

The "UI for AI" Vision

Copilot Chat as a unified interface for all AI agents (homegrown, product, and Microsoft).

"Better together" positioning — not replacing, existing investments.

Integration Scenarios & Technical Feasibility

Strategies to connect homegrown and product AI agents to Copilot Chat using connectors, APIs, and Copilot Studio.

Flexibility to use any LLM and support for multi-agent environments.

Change Management & Adoption

Strategies for minimising disruption and supporting user onboarding.

Addressing organisational concerns: accountability, support, and cost transparency.

Success stories and lessons learned from other large enterprises.

Migration & Coexistence Strategies

Approaches for gradual migration, coexistence, or integration of homegrown solutions.

Emphasis on collaboration, not eradication, of existing tools.

Decision frameworks for when to integrate, coexist, or consider replacement (only if customer expresses need).

Integration paths

How to Bring Your Agents to Microsoft 365 Copilot

  • Agents from Copilot Studio, Azure AI Foundry, or other providers can integrate seamlessly with Microsoft 365 Copilot.
  • Using the Microsoft 365 Agents SDK, you can publish custom agents with your own orchestrators, models, and knowledge sources across Copilot and 15+ channels.
Copilot Studio Agents
Azure AI Foundry agents
GCP/AWS/OpenAI/3rd Party agents
1-click feature · Publish
REST API (1-click feature)
Microsoft 365 Agents SDK
Copilot Studio

MCP Server

Microsoft 365 Agent SDK
Microsoft 365 Copilot
The takeaway

You don't have to choose between your existing AI investments and Microsoft 365 Copilot. Bring them together — let users start in the place they already work, and reach all your agents through a single, governed interface. Pick the integration path that matches your team's skills: 1-click publish for Copilot Studio agents, REST API for Foundry, and the Microsoft 365 Agents SDK when you need full control across 15+ channels.

Bring agents to Copilot Publish Foundry agent to Copilot Microsoft 365 Agents SDK

Copilot Studio · Foundry · Agents SDK — and the comprehensive agent platform

Three valid paths to the same outcome — pick the one your team's skills match. Then see how the whole platform layers together: UI, capabilities, channels, tooling and agent types.

6 min

Three integration paths

How to bring your agent to M365 Copilot Chat

Homegrown AI path
Azure AI Foundry agents GCP / AWS / OpenAI / 3rd Party agents
Feature Copilot Studio Foundry Agents SDK
Development approach Low-code Low-code or Pro-code Pro-code
Tooling Copilot Studio UI Foundry Portal or Visual Studio Code / Visual Studio with Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit Visual Studio Code / Visual Studio with Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit
Publishing My organization My organization · ISV / store My organization · ISV / store · 10+ channels
Channels Microsoft 365 Copilot, Teams, partner apps, mobile apps and custom websites Microsoft 365 Copilot and Teams (other channels require custom integration) Microsoft 365 Copilot, Teams, partner apps, mobile apps and custom websites
Productivity Individual Individual Group
Orchestrator Copilot Studio Bring your own (e.g. Semantic Kernel or LangChain) Bring your own (e.g. Semantic Kernel, LangChain)
AI Models Copilot Studio Foundry OpenAI or custom models Any model of your choice
Supported programming languages Not applicable (low-code) Python, C# C#, JavaScript, Python
Note

Agents from Copilot Studio, Azure AI Foundry, or other providers can integrate seamlessly with Microsoft 365 Copilot.

Using the Microsoft 365 Agents SDK, you can publish custom agents with your own orchestrators, models, and knowledge sources across Copilot and 15+ channels.

Two surfaces, one outcome

Use the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit & SDK when your developers want to ship agents like normal apps — same VS Code / Visual Studio workflow, branching, releases. Use Azure AI Foundry when you want to bring any model, plug in Azure AI Search, and govern the whole agent lifecycle with built-in evaluations, monitoring and observability. Both paths flow into the same Microsoft 365 Copilot experience for your users.

Agents Toolkit Agents SDK · GitHub Azure AI Foundry Azure AI Agent Service
Phase 2 of 4 · Art of the Possible

Art of the Possible

Walk the maturity spectrum live. Six hero demos across Retrieve, Reason, Operate and BYOA so the customer can place their own ideas on the curve.

Readiness Demos Assess Plan
01

Retrieve

Zero-friction value — 5 minutes to a working agent.

  • SharePoint Agents — ACL-aware retrieval
  • Citations & site-level approved agents
02

Reason

Analyst-grade work without leaving M365 Copilot.

  • Researcher — deep reasoning + web/work grounding
  • Analyst — o3-mini + Python (shows the code)
  • Copilot Studio Lite — Marketing Agent
03

Operate

The “wow” tier — trigger + reasoning + tools + autonomy.

  • Agent Builder — Employee IT Agent (VS Code)
  • Autonomous — HR triage + ServiceNow ticket
  • Autonomous — PC Request & approvals
04

BYOA

Surface existing agent investments inside Copilot.

  • Existing Copilot Studio agent recording
  • Existing Teams agent recording
  • Azure AI Foundry agent recording

Art of the Possible — six hero demos across the maturity spectrum

Walk the customer up the maturity spectrum so they can place their own ideas on the curve. Pick a small set of demos, leave plenty of space for ideation.

The maturity spectrum

From "find an answer" to "the agent runs the workflow"

1Retrieve
2Act
3Reason
4Operate Autonomously
SharePoint Agents
Where · in M365 Copilot & SharePoint
Prebuilt Agents + Tools
Where · Researcher · Analyst · Agent Store
Copilot Studio Lite
Where · low-code maker, your business data
Agent Builder + Autonomous
Where · Copilot Studio · VS Code · triggers

The full demo line-up

Seven hero scenarios — pick three for the workshop

Retrieve · 1 of 7

📄SharePoint Agents

Turn any SharePoint library into a chat agent grounded on that library only — permission-trim & citations built in.

Local guide Hero-demo SharePoint folder
Retrieve + Act · 2 of 7

🛍Prebuilt Agents — Researcher / Analyst

Show analyst-grade reasoning over web + work data, with chain-of-thought and Python you can read.

Local guide Hero-demo SharePoint folder
Reason · 3 of 7

📣Copilot Studio Lite — Marketing Agent

A low-code agent for makers: scoped instructions, custom tone, knowledge over a SharePoint marketing library.

Local guide Marketing Agent — Setup and Demo Guide.docx
Act · 4 of 7

👨‍💻Agent Builder — Employee IT Agent

Author an agent in VS Code with the M365 Agents Toolkit: tools, prompt management, debug-then-deploy.

Local guide Agent demo setup guide — EmployeeITAgent.docx
Operate · 5 of 7

🤖Autonomous Agent — HR Scenario

An email-triggered HR agent that reads the request, plans the work, calls a tool and posts back to the user.

Local guide HR Scenario Autonomous Agent Deployment Guide.docx
Operate · 6 of 7

💻Autonomous Agent — PC Request

Request a new PC by chat: multi-step approvals handled by the agent, then a confirmation back to the requester.

Local guide Autonomous Agents — PC Request.docx
BYOA · 7 of 7

🔄BYOA — three short recordings

Foundry agent in M365 Copilot · Copilot Studio agent published to Teams · Teams as the channel for a custom-engine agent.

Local assets AI Foundry · Copilot Studio · Teams

Demo-day rule of three

3

Don't show all six demos — show three

Pick 1 Retrieve (SharePoint Agents) · 1 Reason (Studio Lite or Researcher) · 1 Operate (Autonomous HR). Comprehension drops sharply after demo #3 and the workshop loses its ideation time. Keep the others as references — share the local guides for after the session.

How to use this section

Open with the maturity spectrum so the customer sees where each demo lives. Then run your three chosen demos back-to-back — narrate each one as "this is the same building blocks we discussed earlier, just turned up a notch." Close by asking the customer to drop their own ideas on the same spectrum — that's the bridge into the Assessment phase.

Get a CDX tenant SharePoint adoption playbook Copilot Studio sample agents
Retrieve tier · the easiest “wow”

SharePoint Agents

Any SharePoint document library can become a chat agent grounded on that library only — with permissions honoured and citations on every answer. The fastest way to move the customer from “AI is interesting” to “we could ship this next week.”

01/06 Demo

Demo flow

5 minutes from blank library to working agent

  1. Open a SharePoint site populated with Contoso documents.
  2. Click Add an agent in the document-library command bar.
  3. Choose the library, accept the auto-generated description, save.
  4. Pin the agent to Teams or share it via link.
  5. Ask scoped questions in front of the customer.

Try these

Prompts that always land

Summarise the 2026 wellness benefits.
Compare warranty terms in our standard contract vs. addendum.
List the policies mentioning parental leave and quote the paragraph.
TierRetrieveno Power Platform env required
SetupZero codein-product authoring
SecurityACL-awareSharePoint permissions honoured
Time-to-demo~5 minfrom scratch
Reason tier · prebuilt agents

Researcher & Analyst

Researcher delivers competitive analysis, GTM plans and exec briefs grounded on web + work data. Analyst reasons over your data with chain-of-thought + Python — and shows the code.

02/06 Demo

Demo flow — pick one

Three ways to show analyst-grade work in M365 Copilot

  1. Researcher — competitive analysis or GTM brief on a topic the customer cares about.
  2. Analyst — upload a CSV, ask for trends, view the Python.
  3. Facilitator — Teams meeting summary in real time (alternative if you have a meeting open).

Try these

Researcher prompts that wow

Build a competitive analysis on the top 5 vendors in [the customer’s market], with citations.
Summarise the last 12 months of [topic] research and identify 3 strategic implications for us.
Draft a GTM strategy for [product], grounded on internal docs + public market data.
Modelso3 / GPT-5deep reasoning
GroundingWeb + Workboth surfaces
AnalystPythonruns and shows the code
CostReasoning $budget for it in the Pilot Plan
Reason tier · Copilot Studio Lite

Marketing Agent

Build inside Microsoft 365 Copilot itself. No environment setup. Governed under the user’s M365 Copilot license. The result is a declarative agent distributed via the Microsoft 365 Apps ecosystem.

03/06 Demo

Demo flow

From blank Copilot to a published Marketing Agent

  1. In M365 Copilot click Create an agent.
  2. Describe the agent in natural language — role, tone, knowledge.
  3. Add knowledge: select the Marketing SharePoint site or brand-guidelines doc.
  4. Add starter prompts: launch email · social posts · campaign brief.
  5. Test in the right pane and share with the Marketing M365 group.

Try these

Marketing prompts

Draft a launch email for the new property listing in our brand voice.
Write three LinkedIn posts about [campaign theme] — platform-appropriate tone.
Build a one-page campaign brief: audience, message, channels, KPIs.
AuthoringIn-Copilotbuilt from chat
EffortFew clicksto publish
SecurityACL-awareSharePoint perms honoured
LicensingM365 Copilotno extra licence
Act tier · Agent Builder (Toolkit)

Employee IT Agent

Build in VS Code with the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit (formerly Teams Toolkit). Deployable to Copilot, Teams, web and other channels. Supports both declarative and custom-engine agents.

04/06 Demo

Demo flow

From VS Code project to Copilot-hosted IT helper

  1. Show VS Code project structure from the M365 Agents Toolkit.
  2. Walk through the declarative manifest (instructions, knowledge, tools).
  3. Run locally with the Toolkit’s debug profile (M365 sideload).
  4. Ask the agent IT questions; show invocation from M365 Copilot.
  5. (Optional) Show provisioning + publish to the customer’s tenant.

Try these

IT prompts

My password isn’t working — what should I try first?
I need to install [software] — does our policy allow it?
Show me my last 3 IT tickets and their status.
SurfaceVS Codefamiliar pro-dev tools
Reach15+ channelsvia Agents SDK
ALMSource controlGit-native
Agent typeBothdeclarative or custom-engine
Operate tier · the “wow”

Autonomous Agents — HR + PC Request

The full recipe: trigger + reasoning + knowledge + tools + autonomy. The HR agent triages email without a user prompt and creates a ServiceNow ticket on escalation. The PC Request agent lets employees self-serve a new laptop with multi-step approvals.

05/06 Demo
✉

HR Scenario — build outline

  1. New agent in Copilot Studio (agent template).
  2. Add Contoso Employee Handbook + Wellness Benefits as Knowledge.
  3. Add the ServiceNow Microsoft Copilot Connector as a Tool.
  4. Instructions: answer from knowledge; escalate to ServiceNow when human action is needed.
  5. Trigger: scheduled, or Outlook new email to hr@contoso.com.
  6. Add escalation paths (Teams channel notification).
💻

PC Request — build outline

  1. New agent. Knowledge = Laptop_Description.docx.
  2. Tools = approval flow (Teams Approvals) + optional ServiceNow / Intune.
  3. Instructions: gather requirements (role, mobility, OS), match SKU, request approval, return tracking ID.
  4. Trigger: invocation from M365 Copilot or scheduled inbox sweep.
TriggerEmail or scheduleno human prompt
ActionServiceNowvia Microsoft Copilot Connector
AutonomyEnd-to-endruns without a user
AuditActivity mapin Copilot Studio
Talking points

An agent = trigger + reasoning + knowledge + tools + autonomy, all under Copilot Control System governance. The HR demo is the most-used customer pitch because it touches all five layers in one story.

BYOA · three short recordings

Bring Your Own Agent

Use these recordings when the customer already has an agent investment and wants to surface it inside Microsoft 365 Copilot — your job is to validate the existing investment and show it can collaborate, not be replaced.

06/06 Demo
Recording 1

Existing Copilot Studio Agent BYOA

A custom agent built in Copilot Studio is published to and discoverable from M365 Copilot’s agents pane.

What it shows Publishing · manifest · Copilot Control System governance.
Recording 2

Existing Teams Agent BYOA

A Teams app or bot is upgraded to a Copilot agent surface.

What it shows Appears in the Copilot agents pane and can be invoked in chat.
Recording 3

Azure AI Foundry BYOA

An agent built in Azure AI Foundry (code-first, custom orchestration / models) is exposed as a Copilot agent.

What it shows Useful for IP-heavy or model-customised scenarios.

Folder: Bring Your Own Agent (BYOA) content/ — each recording is short (under 3 minutes) so you can drop one in mid-conversation when a customer mentions an existing chatbot or AI investment.

Customer signal

If the customer says “we already built a chatbot in [tool] last year”, lead with the matching BYOA recording. It validates their investment and shows that Microsoft is a collaboration, not eradication story.

Phase 3 of 4 · Assessment

Assessment

Turn conversation into a prioritised scenario backlog. Discover, envision, prioritise — and feed straight into the Pilot Plan.

Readiness Demos Assess Plan
01

Discovery

Listen first — capture the pain, then the systems behind it.

  • 9-step listening checklist
  • Business challenges & persona pain points
  • Persona template — IT, Knowledge Workers, HR
  • Technical discovery questionnaire
02

Envision

Brainstorm scenarios; turn them into well-defined use cases.

  • 8-step ideation checklist
  • Use-case discussion template
  • Define Agent Use Case (As… / I want… / Using…)
  • Feature mapping — IT Service Desk example
03

Prioritise

Rank candidates and pick the top two for the Pilot Plan.

  • Value × Complexity matrix
  • Top-2 priority extensibility scenarios
  • Prioritised scenarios summary
04

Roadmap

Commit the path to value before leaving the room.

  • AI Transformation Roadmap
  • Owner, date, risks per next step
  • Hand-off into the Pilot Plan

AI Agents Assessment — Discovery + Envisioning

Turn the conversation into a prioritised scenario backlog. Two phases, four outcomes, one Pilot Plan that flows straight into Module 3.

What this module delivers

Module 2 · Assessment at a glance

Objectives

What we set out to do

  • Identify business challenges
  • Identify persona pain points
  • Understand existing systems & processes
  • Ideate possible scenarios
Outcomes

What we walk away with

An Agents Solution Pilot Plan that feeds directly into Module 3 — ready scenarios, owners and target dates.

Audience

Who needs to be in the room

IT Admins · Security Team · BDMs · Project Owner. Bring at least one persona for each function in scope.

Duration

How long it takes

One day — or split across our 3-session workshop format. Discovery ≈ 1.5 h · Envisioning ≈ 2 h.

Two-phase structure

Discovery then Envisioning — in that order

Phase 1

Discovery

  • Business Discovery — listen, capture pain, map systems
  • Technical Discovery — data sources, connectors, environments
→
Phase 2

Envisioning

  • Build Use Cases — brainstorm, define, hypothesise prompts
  • Prioritise Use Cases — plot on Value × Complexity
The golden rule

During Discovery: listen. Do not offer solutions yet. Use Whiteboard or Mural — pen and ink, not slides. The temptation to jump straight to “we could do X with Copilot Studio” derails the customer’s articulation of their actual pain. The solutions emerge in Envisioning, once the canvas is full.

Discovery — capture business + persona + technical challenges

Listen first. Three artefacts: a 9-step listening checklist, a workflow chain for each pain point, and a persona mind-map that becomes the table you take into Envisioning.

Listening checklist

9 steps to a complete Discovery

01Know your customer.
02Listen to your customer.
03Discover business objectives, challenges, stakeholders & outcome.
04Identify where the customer is TODAY.
05Discover business challenges.
06Discover persona pain points.
07Discover the applications currently in use and the data sources.
08Use the discovery business questionnaire (Excel · Discovery tab).
09Document the results.

Identify the WHAT?

Walk the existing workflow, step by step

Identify the WHAT?
Ticket update from helpdesk
→
Employee call or email helpdesk
→
Helpdesk searches for the ticket
→
Helpdesk responds in 4 hours

Example: Ticket Updates Workflow

For each persona pain point, capture the existing workflow as boxes & arrows. The workflow becomes the candidate scenario in Envisioning.

Identify Persona Challenges

A mind-map per persona — 4 challenges around 1 central node

What are the challenges the persona Knowledge Workers encounter when they need a ticket update?

Deflected requests
Lack of digital collaboration & communication
Knowledge Workers
Search time
Delays in updates by Help Desk

What are the challenges the persona Help Desk encounter when they need a ticket update?

High number of incoming calls
Lack of digital collaboration & communication
Help Desk
High number of emails
Time-consuming reply to communication

What are the challenges the persona HR encounter when they need to handle requests?

Challenges in finding answers
Decreased engagement
HR
Low employee satisfaction
Low HR efficiency

Defined Personas / Functions and Pain Points

The table you take into Envisioning

Persona / Function Job Challenges / Pain points Current Apps / Data Sources / Connectors
IT Ticketing process · change-management comms · Help Desk Support (captured during discovery) (captured during discovery)
Knowledge Workers Content search · review & document collaboration (captured during discovery) (captured during discovery)
HR Interviews · benefits / payroll info · Vacation Assistant (captured during discovery) (captured during discovery)
Persona pain examples to spark conversation

Knowledge workers: deflected requests · lack of digital collaboration · search time · delays in updates. Help Desk: high call volume · large email volume · time-consuming replies. HR: challenges finding answers · decreased engagement · low employee satisfaction.

Envisioning — brainstorm, build, prioritise use cases

Three artefacts: a kanban board for the brainstorm, a Define Agent Use Case template for each candidate, and an Agent Configuration card for the chosen ones. Define the top-2 priority extensibility scenarios — max.

Template · Define Use Case

Brainstorm 4 columns wide — Goals → Scenarios → Prompts → Data

Define Goals
Optimise Service Desk
Reduce call volume
Improve support experience
Brainstorm Scenarios
Deflect call volumemore self-service via M365 Copilot, i.e. Ticket Status
Reduce issue research timesuse Copilot to reason over ServiceNow KB, external M365, SP, Email, Chat
Eliminate KB article gapsuse Copilot to write KBs
Reduce Language Barrierstranslate KBs and support live agents with translation
Shrink the service cataloguse Copilot to replace existing chatbots
Hypothesize Prompts
List ServiceNow tickets opened by Jane Doe • What is the status of ticket #1234?
Summarise emails with <label> from the last two weeks • What Microsoft & ServiceNow KBs are related to their issue
List all service-now tickets closed in the week, for each Sub-list the related KBs • Using the notes from ServiceNow issue 123 create a KB using template B
List all KBs related to password reset • How do I reset a password for an employee in Germany in the US • Translate the last response into German
How do I reset my password? • Are there currently any network issues impacting me? • How do I turn off my VPN?
Identify Data Sources
ServiceNow Tickets · ServiceNow KB
ServiceNow KB · Web
ServiceNow KB · ServiceNow Tickets
ServiceNow KB
ServiceNow KB

Example 1 · IT Self-help — a complete fill of the brainstorm canvas.

Template · Define Agent Use Case

One canvas per agent — the user story + the value calculation

As someone in…
[role / function]
I want to…
Onboard new employees and provide training.
Using…
[agent name]
I'll know this is
successful when…

The value AI Agent provided me in completing this job (Functional, Emotional, Social)

Time savingHours/week spent
×
Efficiency improvement %
×
# Unique workers
→
Hours / week saved

Finalize Use Case

Agent Configuration — the three things every agent needs

Instructions
Who is the agent? Role, tone, scope & the things it should never do. Pull straight from the user story above (As someone in… I want to… Using…).
Knowledge
What does it ground on? SharePoint sites, M365 Copilot Connectors, Dataverse, Web search — lifted from the Identify Data Sources column of the kanban.
Actions
What can it do? Power Platform connectors, API plugins, MCP servers, Agent Flows. Map each scenario from the brainstorm to a concrete tool.
Workshop delivery guide (Loop)

Identify High Value Use Cases — prioritise & roadmap

Two questions guide every scenario: Is it obtainable? · Will it be widely used? Then plot on Value × Complexity. Pick 1–3 scenarios for the first Pilot Plan.

Tips for prioritizing scenarios

Four decision criteria — in this order

Decision criteria
✨

Magic factor — does the agent feel like magic to the user?

🎯

Is it obtainable? — do we have the data, system access and skills?

👥

Will it be widely used? (how many people)

📈

Measurable impact — hours saved, % deflection, $ value.

The matrix

Plot every candidate on Value × Complexity

Complexity
Low Value
High Complexity
High Value
High Complexity
Low Value
Low Complexity
High Value
Low Complexity
Value
AutonomousAgents
TaskAgents
RetrievalAgents

Example fill

A typical first wave — IT, HR and Marketing

Complexity
Low Value
High Complexity
High Value
High Complexity
AI Agent for IT
Low Value
Low Complexity
AI Agent for HR AI Agent for Marketing
High Value
Low Complexity
Value
AutonomousAgents
TaskAgents
RetrievalAgents

Start in the High Value / Low Complexity quadrant — those become the first 1–2 pilot scenarios.

Prioritised scenarios summary

The output that goes into the Pilot Plan

Scenario Function / Persona Solution Goals Business impact Priority → Pilot Plan
IT Self-help Knowledge Workers · Helpdesk Declarative agent + ServiceNow connector Reduce SLA time · deflect call volume ~30% deflection · 200 hrs/wk saved High Yes — Wave 1
HR Vacation Assistant HR · All employees Declarative agent + Dataverse Self-serve balance & requests 4,000 employees · lower HR queue High Yes — Wave 1
Marketing Agent Marketing Studio Lite agent + Marketing SP site Faster campaign briefs & copy ~15 hrs/wk per copywriter Med Wave 2

AI Transformation Roadmap

Next steps with owners and dates

Next Step Owner Date Description Risk
SAM oversharing scan SP Admin + 2 weeks Run SharePoint Advanced Management oversharing reports across pilot sites. Sensitivity-label gaps — mitigated by Purview
Pilot kickoff workshop Project Owner + 3 weeks Half-day session to confirm Wave 1 scenarios, success metrics and adoption plan. N/A
Build IT Self-help agent Maker / IT + 5 weeks Build, test and pilot in Copilot Studio with the ServiceNow connector. Connector throttling on first deploy
Adoption review Adoption Lead + 8 weeks Review usage telemetry, MAU, ticket-deflection rate; feed into Wave 2 plan. N/A
Pick 1–3 to feed into the Pilot Plan

Don’t exceed two priority scenarios in your first Pilot Plan — fewer scenarios shipped well beats a wide list shipped late. The Excel Prioritization Tasks Sheet in the kit makes this concrete with scoring on Value (impact, alignment, reach, frequency) × Feasibility (data, system access, security, change-management).

Phase 4 of 4 · Pilot Plan

Pilot Plan

The deliverable that closes the workshop and locks the next engagement. Use cases, requirements, governance, and the transformation journey.

Readiness Demos Assess Plan
01

Use Cases & Participants

Lock the names and the scenarios in the same session.

  • Top-2 priority use cases & KPIs
  • Define AI Agent Use Case — HR onboarding example
  • Participants — customer + Microsoft roster
02

Requirements

The four boxes the customer must check before pilot.

  • Licenses — M365 Copilot & Copilot Studio
  • Knowledge sources — SP, Connectors
  • Environment setup — makers vs developers
03

Governance

What the security team needs to see signed off.

  • M365 Copilot Agents controls
  • Copilot Studio & Power Platform controls
  • Microsoft Purview — 8 capabilities
  • Copilot Connectors governance
04

Next Steps & Practical

What happens after the workshop — and the artefacts to take away.

  • Up-to-3 next actions & Value Realisation
  • AI Transformation Journey & Microsoft offerings
  • Decision tree & reference architectures
  • Anti-patterns & pre-pilot checklist

AI Agents Pilot Plan — the deliverable that closes the workshop

A customer-ready report — delivered after the Closeout meeting. Captures every workshop outcome and locks the next engagement with names, dates and Y/N decisions.

Overview

What’s inside the Pilot Plan deck

01 Overview & Participants
02 Assessment Findings
03 Technical Requirements
04 Governance Controls
05 AI Transformation Journey
06 Recommendation & Next Steps

Source: AI Agents Workshop Pilot Plan Report.pptx — presented by your Cloud Solution Architect.

What feeds in here

Everything from Module 2 · Assessment — the prioritised scenarios, persona challenges and use-case definitions — lands directly in this report. The Pilot Plan turns those findings into a customer-ready commitment with owners, dates and risks.

Participants & priority use cases — top 2

Lock the names and the scenarios in the same session — both go on the cover of the Pilot Plan deck.

Participants

Who needs to be on the deck cover

Live workshop notes — click any cell to edit, then export the page or screenshot for the deck.

Customer

RoleName
BDM
Project Manager
IT Admin
Sponsor

Add or remove rows as needed.

Microsoft

RoleName
Cloud Solution Architect
Cloud Solution Architect
CSAM
Solution Engineer

Add or remove rows as needed.

Priority Use Cases (top 2)

Lock the scenarios that go into the Pilot Plan

Dept. Scenario Title Value Statement Use Case Business Value Extensibility Type Connector Name No of Users Personas Sponsors Business KPI KPI future Impact Priority

Have the executive sponsor’s name in the deck before the session ends.

Example · Define AI Agent Use Case — Onboarding HR Agent

The template you fill out together with the customer for each priority use case — same shape as the Envisioning canvas, but with the worked numbers in.

ExampleWorked example

Onboarding HR Agent — Store manager

This canvas is shown pre-filled as an example — click any field to overwrite with the customer’s scenario.
As someone in…
Store manager
I want to…
Onboard new employees and provide training.
Using…
Onboarding HR Agent — ensure new hires have a seamless and welcoming start. Greet new team members, assist with technical issues, answer questions, provide essential company resources, explain the company culture; list training courses and guide new hires through HR processes.
I’ll know this is
successful when…
I get a list of information that is relevant to my job with accurate references / links to items.

The value AI Agent provided me in completing this job:
Functional: save time, increase quality.
Emotional: relief from reduced negative feedback, see team morale rise, employee appreciation.
Social: improved customer satisfaction, retain workers.

Time savingHours/week spent
×
Efficiency improvement %
×
# Unique workers
→
Hours / week saved

Why we measure

Four value outcomes — pick what matters to the customer

Outcome

⚙️ Operational efficiency

Tasks completed faster; fewer hand-offs; backlog reduction.

Outcome

😊 Customer satisfaction

Faster response times, higher first-contact resolution, better CSAT/NPS.

Outcome

📈 Revenue growth

More deals advanced, faster proposal cycles, higher close rates.

Outcome

👥 Employee satisfaction

Less repetitive work, better tooling, lower turnover risk.

Capture hours/week spent × efficiency improvement × # workers to size the business case in the value pill above.

Technical Requirements — basic

The four boxes the customer must check before the pilot starts. Y/N answers only — no “we’ll figure it out”.

📝

Basic Requirements

Licenses, knowledge sources, environment setup
Click any cell to edit. Click the pink Y/N pill to cycle Y / N / pending status with the customer.
Requirements Description Resource Completion Status
Licences Microsoft 365 Copilot Licenses
Users to have Microsoft 365 E3, Microsoft 365 E5, Microsoft 365 Business Standard or Microsoft 365 Business Premium licenses		 Get started with Microsoft Copilot for Microsoft 365 | Microsoft Learn Y/N
Licences Copilot Studio Licences Copilot Studio licensing — Microsoft Copilot Studio | Microsoft Learn Y/N
Knowledge Source Decide which knowledge source the AI Agent needs to connect to. Knowledge sources examples: files, folders, and sites from SharePoint and Microsoft Copilot Connectors. Microsoft Copilot Connectors overview Y/N
Environment Setup Makers, Users, Developers
Decide who in the organisation is required to build agents — makers or developers.		 Declarative agents overview Y/N
Pre-pilot blockers

Most pilot delays trace back to licensing not assigned or environment not provisioned. Get the IT admin to commit to dates for both before leaving the room.

Governance Controls — Microsoft 365 Copilot Agents & Copilot Studio

Two governance tables, both with Y/N status columns the customer must complete.

Microsoft 365 Copilot Agents Technical Requirements

AI Agent Governance Controls
RequirementsDescriptionResourceCompletion Status
Agent management control policies decision Identify agents access policies for users:
• Make a Copilot agent available to specific users or groups.
• Block or unblock agents for the complete organization.
• Deploy or remove agents for the complete organization or specific users or groups.		   Y/N
Power Platform Admin Center Developer and resource access policies decision
Manage access to Copilot Studio		   Y/N
Power Platform Admin Center Identify if you require to create a pay-as-you-go billing plan for agents in Copilot Chat.   Y/N
Microsoft 365 Admin Center Identify Microsoft 365 administrators to carry out management tasks such as agent access controls, publishing, deployment, measurement, and users controls. Manage agents for Microsoft 365 Copilot in Integrated Apps — Microsoft 365 admin | Microsoft Learn Y/N
View message consumption Identify who can have access to view message consumption.   Y/N

Copilot Studio Technical Requirements

AI Agent Governance Controls
RequirementsDescriptionResourceCompletion Status
Power Platform Admin Center Developer and resource access policies decision
Manage access to Copilot Studio		   Y/N
Power Platform Environment Controls Managed Environments · DLP · Maker access   Y/N
Manage Copilot agents

Microsoft Purview — 8 governance capabilities

If the customer has a CISO, this is the table they want signed off. Each row maps a Purview capability to the AI-agent risk it mitigates.

Microsoft Purview Technical Requirements

AI Agent Governance Controls
RequirementsDescriptionResourceCompletion Status
DSPM AI Data Security Posture Management (DSPM) for AI enables customers to gain visibility into agent usage and assess data risks. DSPM for AI Y/N
Data Loss Protection Data Loss Prevention (DLP) helps mitigate data exfiltration risks in agents using SharePoint grounding data.   Y/N
Information Protection Information Protection provides a comprehensive view of the potential risks to an organisation’s sensitive data. Microsoft Purview Information Protection Y/N
Insider Risk Management Help detect risky activities across agents, such as receiving an unusual number of agent responses containing sensitive data. Get started with insider risk management Y/N
Communication Compliance Help prevent agent interactions that may result in a security or compliance incident, and safeguard sensitive information in AI interactions. Communication compliance Y/N
eDiscovery Agent interactions can be reviewed in eDiscovery for investigations and litigations. eDiscovery solutions Y/N
Audit Audit logs record interactions with agents to facilitate investigations whenever necessary. Auditing solutions in Microsoft Purview Y/N
Data Lifecycle Management With Data Lifecycle Management, security admins can set retention and deletion policies for their agent interactions. Microsoft Purview Data Lifecycle Management Y/N

Microsoft Copilot Connectors — governance controls

Required if the pilot uses any Copilot Connectors (ServiceNow, Jira, Confluence, Salesforce…).

Microsoft Copilot Connectors Technical Requirements

AI Agent Governance Controls
RequirementsDescriptionResourceCompletion Status
Manage Microsoft Copilot connectors Identify the admins who will manage the required Copilot Connectors. Microsoft 365 Copilot connectors overview Y/N
Authentication Method Decide on the Authentication Method of the Copilot Connectors in scope. Set up Microsoft 365 Copilot connectors Y/N
Copilot Connectors ACL and External Group permissions management for Microsoft Copilot connectors data sources. Use external groups to manage permissions Y/N
Microsoft Copilot connectors limits Understand the API limitation of connection, schema, group, item ingestion, and throttling. Microsoft 365 Copilot connectors API limits Y/N
Pre-pilot security & governance checklist

11 items to tick off before kickoff: tenant readiness · data hygiene (SAM oversharing scan) · identity (Entra app reg) · DLP · Power Platform managed environment · connector inventory · audit · Responsible AI · cost (run estimator) · operations runbook · decommission plan.

Next Steps & Value Realisation — the closing tables

The two tables that close the Pilot Plan deck — short, owner-named, with dates next to each commitment.

The blank rows are yours to fill in with the customer. The rows tagged Example are reference patterns from past engagements.
ActionResponsibilityCompletion dateNotes

ExampleRequest security review for integrating external data with Copilot Connectors Security Team MM/DD/YYYY Ensure compliance with company policies and Microsoft security guidelines.

ExampleValue Realisation

Pre-filled as an example timeline — click any cell to type the customer’s real scenarios.

HR Scenario 1, Scenario 2 Scenario 3
IT Scenario 1 Scenario 2 Scenario 3
Marketing Scenario 1, Scenario 2
Sales Scenario 1, Scenario 2 Scenario 3
Service

AI Transformation Journey & the next Microsoft engagement

Where the Pilot Plan ends and the next engagement begins — owners, dates, risks, and the Microsoft Unified offering that fits.

AI Transformation Journey

Next steps with owners, dates and risks

Live notes — use the blank rows to draft commitments with the customer. Example rows below show the format.
Next stepOwnerCompletion DateDescriptionRisks, Blocker, or N/A
ExamplePurchase new Microsoft 365 Copilot, Copilot Studio, Copilot for Sales licenses Customer 07/15/2025 Customer to acquire licensing for 3,000 users Unplanned Cost

AI Agents Workshop delivery guide AI Agent Workshop datasheet
Closing thought · Stakeholder alignment

A successful pilot needs four groups aligned from day one: Business stakeholders (the use case & ROI), Technical teams (the build & integration), Governance & compliance (Purview, DSPM, Responsible AI), and Security (identity, DLP, conditional access). The Pilot Plan is the artefact that gets all four to commit to the same scenario, the same KPIs, and the same timeline.

Picking the right path for your agent — a simple decision aid

Two simple aids you can use right in the room with us: a quick decision grid that helps you choose the right product based on what your agent should do and where your people work — plus the 4 common patterns that almost every idea falls into.

Decision grid

Pick the right Microsoft tool for your idea

Where will people use it? → ↓ What should it do?
In Microsoft 365 Copilotyour employees, every day
In your own apps & channelsweb, mobile, Teams, more
With external userscustomers, partners, citizens
With your own AI modelopen-source or fine-tuned
📖
Answer questions
find & summarise content
Best fit
SharePoint Agents
or Copilot Studio Lite No code, respects your existing permissions
Copilot Studio
declarative agent Same agent, also published to Teams & channels
Copilot Studio
+ web channel Anonymous or signed-in external users
Probably overkill Talk to us first Q&A scenarios rarely need a custom model
⚡
Take an action
do a few things in your systems
Best fit
Copilot Studio
declarative agent + tools A connector or API plugin per task
M365 Agents Toolkit
+ SDK For your developer team in VS Code
Copilot Studio
+ web/mobile channel Branded portal or in your own app
Azure AI Foundry
+ BYOA Your own AI brain, your own tools
🤖
Run on its own
starts on a trigger, no human prompt
Best fit
Copilot Studio
custom-engine + triggers Email or schedule starts it; every run is logged
Agents SDK
+ Agent Flows When the steps need to be exact, every time
Copilot Studio
web-hook trigger An external form or system kicks it off
Foundry Agent Service
+ Logic Apps Full control over how the agent runs
🔗
Several agents working together
specialists handing off the work
Best fit
M365 Copilot
+ Connected Agents Researcher · Analyst · your own agents in one place
Agents SDK
+ Semantic Kernel When your developers need to write the choreography
Copilot Studio
multi-agent Same idea, surfaced inside your own app
Foundry
+ AutoGen / LangChain Agents talking to agents using your own AI models

Find the row that describes what your agent should do, then look across to the column for where your people will use it. The cells tagged Best fit are the easiest place to start — the others are good options if your situation calls for something different.

Common shapes of agent

The 4 patterns most ideas turn out to be

A Pattern A

Find & summarise

The agent only reads from your content. It pulls answers from a SharePoint library and a connector or two, and shows the source on every reply.

Good fits: HR FAQs, IT knowledge base, policy questions, contract look-up.
B Pattern B

Get something done

The agent does a task in your systems on the user’s behalf — with a quick confirmation card so the person stays in control.

Good fits: approvals, raising a ticket, updating a CRM record, checking a status.
C Pattern C

Run quietly in the background

The agent starts on its own when something happens (an email, a schedule), thinks it through, takes an action, and escalates if it needs a human.

Good fits: HR triage, claims intake, inbox monitoring, scheduled compliance checks.
D Pattern D

A team of agents

Several agents work together on one task — a Researcher gathers, an Analyst crunches the numbers, your custom agent applies your business logic.

Good fits: cross-team workflows, deal qualification, M&A research, board prep.
How we’ll use these together

In the workshop, we’ll use the grid to pick the Microsoft tool that fits your idea, and the 4 patterns to size the build and call out the components we’ll need (knowledge sources, tools, triggers). Together, the two give us a clear, plain-English plan you can take back to your team.

Things to watch out for & the pre-pilot readiness check

A short list of mistakes we’ve seen play out in other rooms — and a quick 13-point readiness check we can tick off together before you start your first pilot.

Things to watch out for

7 traps to avoid — and the simpler path

# The trap Why it hurts What works better
01 Trying to do everything at once The agent gets vague, hard to test, and people lose trust quickly. Start small — one team and one job to do — then grow as confidence grows.
02 Pointing the agent at a wide-open SharePoint site It can quote documents people shouldn’t see — an oversharing surprise. Run the SharePoint oversharing report first and tighten access before the agent goes live.
03 Using a search connector for live data Answers can be a few hours old — users see yesterday’s information. Use a tool / connector instead so the agent reads the system in real time.
04 Putting all the rules in one big prompt Hard to test and easy to break the next time the model is updated. Split it: instructions for personality, flows for the must-do steps, tools for the actions.
05 Going fully automated before the basic version works Wastes capacity and frustrates users when the agent gets things wrong. Start with a chat-style agent, see how it does, then turn on triggers when it’s reliably right.
06 Letting the agent answer without sources One wrong answer with no link to back it up — and trust is gone. Make citations a must in the agent’s instructions: no source, no answer.
07 Forgetting to plan for cost Surprise invoices — especially if the agent runs on its own many times a day. Use the Copilot Credit estimator up front, set capacity alerts, review usage in the first weeks.

Pre-pilot readiness check

13 things to confirm before kickoff

🔒

Your data & users

6 items · tick each box together as you confirm them
0 / 6
⚙

Your platform & team

7 items · same idea — tick as we agree
0 / 7
How we’ll use this together

In the workshop we’ll go through the traps first — they often spark a “wait, we were about to do that” moment. Then we’ll run through the readiness check live: tick what you can confirm today, and leave the rest with your IT & security teams to close before kickoff. The progress bar gives us a clear “ready to go” signal we can include in your Pilot Plan.

A printable hand-out

This page is the quickest way to take everything home. Print it (the search box is hidden in print mode and full URLs are appended for paper readers), or send the link to your team. Every Microsoft Learn URL referenced anywhere in the workshop is here, organised by module and topic.

The Frontier — from AI assistants to AI co-workers

Where the agent story goes next — and what it means for your organisation. This is the conversation to have with executives after the Pilot Plan is signed.

The shift

From AI assistants to AI co-workers

Assistants help you draft, summarise, look things up. Co-workers act on behalf of the team — they understand the goal, do the work and bring back something you can ship. Today’s pilot is tomorrow’s first co-worker.

🚀

The shift

“AI assistant” → “AI co-worker”.

Microsoft’s Frontier Firm vision — today’s pilot is tomorrow’s first co-worker.
👨

For workers

Every employee gets a teammate, not just a tool.

Personal agents from day one · Researcher-grade tools for every role · routine work safely delegated.
💼

For managers

Orchestrate hybrid human + agent teams.

Span of control re-defined · agent capacity becomes a planning input · new surface for KPIs.
🏢

For the organisation

Three metrics that move at scale.

Capacity · Skills · Time — the Frontier Firm is humans + agents on one team.

What is it?

The Frontier Firm — humans and agents on the same team

A Frontier Firm is an organisation where every person works alongside one or more AI agents — the agents take on the routine, repeatable, multi-step work, and the people focus on judgement, relationships and creative leaps.

It’s less “AI as a tool” and more AI as a teammate: agents that understand the goal, do the work, and bring back something the human can act on.

— Microsoft · Frontier Firm vision · 2026
82%
of leaders expect autonomous agents to be a meaningful part of their teams within 18 months
Microsoft Work Trend Index, 2025
3×
productivity uplift reported by early adopters of agent-augmented work in knowledge roles
Microsoft customer studies, 2024–2026
~40%
of a typical knowledge worker’s week is routine, multi-step work — the sweet spot for agents
Industry research aggregate

Why now?

Four shifts that make this real this year, not in five

🧠

Models can reason now

Today’s frontier models can break a request into steps, choose the right tool, and check their own answer.

Why it matters: agents stop being chatbots and start being teammates.
🔌

The platform is plumbed

Microsoft 365 Copilot, Copilot Studio, Connectors, MCP and Agent Flows mean agents can read your data and act on your systems — safely.

Why it matters: you don’t need to glue 10 tools together first.
🛡

Governance is built in

Purview, DSPM for AI, audit, sensitivity labels and DLP cover agents from day one — not as a bolt-on later.

Why it matters: security and compliance can say yes earlier.
💰

The economics finally work

Pay-as-you-go credits, prebuilt agents and low-code authoring mean the cost of trying an idea is hours, not months.

Why it matters: you can pilot & measure before you commit.

How it works

A Frontier team — what each side brings

👨

Humans bringjudgement, context, relationships

  • Set the goal — decide what good looks like.
  • Approve the high-stakes calls — spend, customer responses, hiring.
  • Bring the relationships — trust, empathy, negotiation.
  • Spot “does this look right?” — the gut check the model can’t fake.
  • Improve the agent — coach it with feedback over time.
work together on one team
🤖

Agents bringscale, speed, consistency

  • Do the routine work — triage, lookups, drafts, summaries.
  • Read across data — SharePoint, Dataverse, Connectors, web.
  • Take action — create tickets, update CRM, send drafts.
  • Run on their own — triggered by email, schedule or events.
  • Hand off when stuck — escalate to a human with the context.
Capacityoutput per person grows
Skillsexpert-level help for everyone
Timehours back from routine work

What it looks like

A day in the life — four roles, four agents

💼

Marta, HR business partnerMid-size retailer · 4,000 employees

My HR onboarding agent greets every new joiner, answers their first 50 questions from the handbook, and books their week-one training. I only step in when something doesn’t fit the template — which is now the interesting half of my job.
Onboarding Handbook Q&A Saved 8 hrs / week
👨

Diego, IT helpdesk leadGlobal manufacturer · 18 sites

Our autonomous IT agent watches the inbox, reads ServiceNow, and resolves ~30% of tickets before a human sees them — password resets, VPN fixes, software access. Tickets that need a person come with a one-line summary and the suggested next step.
Email trigger ServiceNow ~30% deflection
📊

Aisha, marketing managerSaaS company · 6 markets

Before a campaign, the Researcher agent pulls competitor pricing & recent press, the Analyst agent sizes the addressable audience from our CRM, and my Marketing agent drafts the launch brief in our brand voice. I review — and ship the same morning.
Multi-agent Researcher + Analyst Days → hours
📊

Rui, finance directorPublic-sector body · 250-page month-end

My finance reporting agent ingests month-end exports, reconciles against last month, flags the variances I should care about, and drafts the board commentary. I review three pages instead of 250 — and the rest of my week is spent on the next quarter, not the last one.
Scheduled trigger Variance flagging Board-ready draft
Where to start your Frontier journey

You don’t become a Frontier Firm by buying it — you become one by shipping your first agent, learning, then shipping the next. The Pilot Plan we agreed today is the first step: pick the use case where the agent will do the most routine work, ship it to a small team, and measure how many hours come back. Repeat. The Frontier is the sum of those small wins.

Your AI Agents cheatsheet — one page to remember everything we covered

A visual map of the workshop. Use it to refresh memory before your next conversation, or hand it to a colleague who couldn’t join. Every concept you’ll need to talk about agents confidently is on this page.

🧠
AI Agents on Microsoft 365 Workshop recap · the four things that matter
📚
Foundations & strategy Module 1 · what an agent actually is
Big idea: Agents = instructions + knowledge + tools. Pick the simplest path that gets the job done.
Microsoft 365 Copilot Copilot Chat Copilot Control System Architecture Enterprise Data Protection Decision guide Declarative vs custom-engine Agent Store Prebuilt agents Researcher / Analyst Facilitator / Interpreter Partner agents
🔧
Build & extend Module 2 · how the maker tracks fit together
Big idea: Studio Lite for users, Copilot Studio for makers, Toolkit + SDK for developers, Foundry for ML teams. Same Copilot, different doors.
Studio Lite (Agent Builder) SharePoint agents Copilot Studio Agents Toolkit Agents SDK Azure AI Foundry Instructions & testing Knowledge sources Copilot Connectors Tools (PP & API) MCP servers Agent flows Triggers Adaptive cards BYOA & Connected Agents
🛡
Govern & secure Module 3 · trust by design, not by chance
Big idea: Three control planes: Microsoft 365 admin (who/what), Power Platform admin (where/how), Microsoft Purview (data & risk). Wire all three before pilot.
M365 admin · licenses Integrated Apps PP admin centre DLP & environments DSPM for AI Information Protection Insider Risk Communication Compliance eDiscovery & Audit Data Lifecycle Responsible AI Oversharing scan
🚀
Adopt & scale From pilot to organisation-wide value
Big idea: Start with two high-value use cases, instrument credits & value, and grow from there. Adoption beats architecture every time.
Top-2 use cases Pilot Plan Credits & estimator Billing & licensing Champions & CoE Readiness checklist Demo tenant (CDX) Hackathon Immersion Workshop Governance Workshop Pilot — Employee Assist Pilot — BPA
❓

Questions worth asking yourself

Conversation starters
  1. Which two use cases would unlock the most measurable value in the next 90 days?
  2. Who owns the agent on day one — an executive sponsor, a product owner, or IT?
  3. Are licences, environments and an oversharing scan in place to start safely?
  4. What’s the simplest build path that meets the requirement — declarative or custom-engine?
  5. How will we measure success — adoption, credits consumed, time saved, or business KPI?
  6. What’s the right next engagement — pilot, hackathon, or governance workshop?
✔

Four things to walk out with

  • 01Top-2 use cases agreed for the Pilot Plan.
  • 02Three named owners on the customer side — sponsor, PM, IT admin.
  • 03Next engagement chosen (Pilot, Hackathon or Governance Workshop) with a target date.
  • 04Readiness checklist assigned to a name — licences, oversharing scan, environment.
🎉
Thank you for spending the day with us. The next great agent in your organisation is the one you’ll start building this week.
Diogo Vida & Guilherme Corte Real
Cloud Solution Architects · Microsoft
